blocks:
Sūrat al-Qaṣaṣ (The Stories), Makkan

Its number of verses is eighty-eight verses. Its points of difference are two verses: طسم ** [is the reading of] the Kufan [reciters], يَسْقُونَ ** [is the reading of] the non-Kufan [reciters]. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) طسم ** (1) طسم** (Ṭā Sīn Mīm)

(٢) تِلْكَ آياتُ الْكِتابِ الْمُبِينِ (2) تِلْكَ آياتُ الْكِتابِ الْمُبِينِ (These are the signs of the manifest Book)

(٣) نَتْلُوا عَلَيْكَ مِنْ نَبَإِ مُوسى وَفِرْعَوْنَ ** (3) نَتْلُوا عَلَيْكَ مِنْ نَبَإِ مُوسى وَفِرْعَوْنَ (We recount to you some of the account of Moses and Pharaoh) some of their account بِالْحَقِ ** (with the truth) establishing the truth (محقّين) **لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (for a people who have faith) because they are the ones who benefit from it.

(٤) إِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ عَلا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (4) إِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ عَلا فِي الْأَرْضِ (Indeed Pharaoh acted arrogantly in the land) the land of Egypt وَجَعَلَ أَهْلَها شِيَعاً ** (and divided its inhabitants into factions) groups (فرقاً) that follow [him/each other] يَسْتَضْعِفُ طائِفَةً مِنْهُمْ ** (oppressing a group among them) and they are the Children of Israel يُذَبِّحُ أَبْناءَهُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيِي نِساءَهُمْ ** (slaughtering their sons and sparing their women) And that was because a soothsayer (كاهن) told him that a child would be born among the Children of Israel at whose hand his kingdom would vanish. And that was due to the extremity of his foolishness (حمقه), for if he believed [the soothsayer], the killing would not avert [the prophecy], and if he disbelieved, then what was his justification (وجهه)? إِنَّهُ كانَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** (Indeed he was one of the agents of corruption) And for that reason, he dared to kill many creatures from the offspring of the Prophets due to a corrupt delusion (تخيّل فاسد).

(٥) وَنُرِيدُ أَنْ نَمُنَّ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (5) And We desired to show favour to those who were abased in the land, that We bestow favor upon them, [describing] the state of one who is being abased, or a narration of a past state. وَنَجْعَلَهُمْ أَئِمَّةً وَنَجْعَلَهُمُ الْوارِثِينَ ** and make them imams, and make them the inheritors, (٦) وَنُمَكِّنَ لَهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (6) and establish them in the land, We grant them authority therein. وَنُرِيَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهامانَ وَجُنُودَهُما مِنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَحْذَرُونَ ** and show Pharaoh and Haman and their hosts what they were wary of concerning them. namely, the loss of their kingdom and their destruction.

It was recited (وقرء) as وَيُرَى (and He shows) with the yāʾ (بالياء) and the raising (رفع) of the nouns (الأسماء). In al-Ghaybah, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), He said: "They are the Family of Muhammad (may the prayers of Allah be upon them). Allah will send their Mahdi after their hardship, and He will grant them might and humiliate their enemies." In Nahj al-Balaghah, He (peace be upon him) said: "The world will surely turn back to us after its aversion, like the turning of a fierce she-camel to its young." And immediately following that, He recited: وَنُرِيدُ أَنْ نَمُنَ ** the Ayah. In al-Kafi: Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) looked at Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) walking and said: "Do you see this one? This one is among those concerning whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَنُرِيدُ أَنْ نَمُنَّ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا ** the Ayah." In al-Ma‘ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله looked at ‘Ali, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them) and wept, saying: 'You are the abased (المستضعفون) after me. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَنُرِيدُ ** the Ayah.'" It was said to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "What is the meaning of that, O son of the Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Its meaning is that you are the Imams after me. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَنُرِيدُ أَنْ نَمُنَّ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَنَجْعَلَهُمْ أَئِمَّةً ** the Ayah." Then He said: "This Ayah applies to us until the Day of Resurrection." In al-Majalis, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "It is for us (لنا) or concerning us (فينا)." In al-Ikmal and al-Ghaybah, when the Qa’im (peace be upon him) was born, He spoke with this Ayah. Al-Qummi said: Allah informed His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله of what Moses and his companions suffered from Pharaoh—of killing and oppression—so that it might be a consolation for him regarding what would befall his Household (may the prayers of Allah be upon them) from his nation. Then, after consoling him, He gave him glad tidings that He would bestow favor upon them after that, make them successors (خلفاء) in the land, Imams over his nation, and return them to the world along with their enemies so that they might take vengeance upon them. So He said: وَنُرِيدُ أَنْ نَمُنَ ** the Ayah. He said: وَنُرِيَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهامانَ وَجُنُودَهُما ** means those who usurped the right of the Family of Muhammad. And His saying: مِنْهُمْ ** means from the Family of Muhammad. ما كانُوا يَحْذَرُونَ ** means of killing and punishment. He said: "If this Ayah had been revealed concerning Moses and Pharaoh, He would have said: وَنُرِيَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهامانَ وَجُنُودَهُما ** minhu (مِنْهُ) ما كانُوا يَحْذَرُونَ ** meaning, from Moses, and He would not have said minhum (مِنْهُمْ). Since His saying preceded: وَنُرِيدُ أَنْ نَمُنَّ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَنَجْعَلَهُمْ أَئِمَّةً , we know that the address is directed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله." In summary, interpreting the narrations reported concerning this as a literal exegesis (تفسير) of the Ayah involves a degree of forced interpretation (التكلّف). He sought to use as evidence sayings of theirs (peace be upon them) which do not indicate what he sought. The correct approach (الصواب) is to interpret the narrations as allegorical interpretation (التأويل), just as in the rest of the narrations reported concerning similar Ayahs. It is known that the pronoun in مِنْهُمْ ** refers back to الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا , meaning the Children of Israel, like the rest of the pronouns. In al-Jawami‘, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "By Him Who sent Muhammad with the truth as a bearer of glad tidings and a warner, the righteous ones (الأبرار) among us, the People of the House (أهل البيت), and their followers (شيعتهم) are in the position of Moses and his followers, and our enemy and their followers are in the position of Pharaoh and his followers."

(٧) وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلى أُمِّ مُوسى أَنْ أَرْضِعِيهِ (7) And We revealed to Moses’ mother, [saying]: ‘Nurse him; as long as you were able to conceal him. فَإِذا خِفْتِ عَلَيْهِ ** but when you fear for him, the sound (of the search), فَأَلْقِيهِ فِي الْيَمِّ ** cast him into the river, into the Nile, وَلا تَخافِي ** and do not fear, for him loss (ضيعة) or hardship (شدّة), وَلا تَحْزَنِي ** nor grieve. over his separation. إِنَّا رَادُّوهُ إِلَيْكِ We will indeed restore him to you soon, in a manner where you will be safe concerning him, وَجاعِلُوهُ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ and We will make him one of the apostles.’

(8)** فَالْتَقَطَهُ آلُ فِرْعَوْنَ لِيَكُونَ لَهُمْ عَدُوًّا وَحَزَناً ** (8) Then Pharaoh’s family picked him up, that he might be an enemy and a sorrow for them.

(This is) a justification for their picking him up, using what was its ultimate consequence and outcome, likening it (the consequence) to the purpose that motivated it. And it was recited with the ḍammah on the ḥāʾ and the sukūn (حُزْناً).

إِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهامانَ وَجُنُودَهُما كانُوا خاطِئِينَ (9) Indeed Pharaoh and Haman and their hosts were sinful.

(٩) ** وَقالَتِ امْرَأَتُ فِرْعَوْنَ ** (9) And Pharaoh’s wife said meaning, to Pharaoh when she took him out of the ark (التابوت): قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍ لِي وَلَكَ (He is) a comfort to my eye and yours. (It is also narrated that she said: He is a comfort to our eye.) In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Abbas: Pharaoh said, "A comfort to your eye, but as for me, no." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By Him in Whose Name the oath is sworn, had Pharaoh acknowledged that he would be ** قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍ ** (a comfort to his eye), just as his wife acknowledged it, Allah would have guided him through him, just as He guided her. But he refused due to the wretchedness (الشقاء) that Allah had decreed upon Him. ** لا تَقْتُلُوهُ عَسى أَنْ يَنْفَعَنا ** Do not kill him; maybe he will benefit us, for in him are signs (مخايل) of blessedness (اليمن) and indications (دلائل) of benefit. ** أَوْ نَتَّخِذَهُ وَلَداً ** or we will adopt him as a son, and we will take him as a son, for he is worthy of it. ** وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** while they were unaware, that he was the one through whom their kingdom would perish.

(١٠) وَأَصْبَحَ فُؤادُ أُمِّ مُوسى فارِغاً ** (10) And the heart of Moses’ mother became void. Void of intellect (صفراً من العقل) due to the fear and confusion (الخوف والحيرة) that overwhelmed her. إِنْ كادَتْ لَتُبْدِي بِهِ ** She almost revealed his matter and his story. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): She almost informed of his news or died, then she restrained herself. لَوْ لا أَنْ رَبَطْنا عَلى قَلْبِها ** (10) Had We not fortified her heart, with patience (الصبر) and steadfastness (الثبات), لِتَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (so that she might be among the faithful): among those who affirm Allah's promise (المصدّقين بوعد الله) or those who trust in His preservation (الواثقين بحفظه). In al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith explaining this story, He said: When she feared the sound (الصوت) for him, Allah Almighty revealed to her: "Make the ark (التابوت), then place him in it, then take it out at night and cast it into the Nile of Egypt (نيل مصر)." So she placed him in the ark, then pushed it into the river (اليمّ). It kept returning to her, and she kept pushing it into the deep water (الغمر). Then the wind struck it and carried it away. When she saw that the water had carried it away, she intended to cry out, but Allah fortified her heart.

(١١) وَقالَتْ لِأُخْتِهِ قُصِّيهِ Follow his trace and seek his news فَبَصُرَتْ بِهِ عَنْ جُنُبٍ from a distance وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ that she was following the track and that she was his sister. (11) And she said to his sister, 'Follow his track.' So she watched him from afar, while they were unaware.

(١٢) وَحَرَّمْنا عَلَيْهِ الْمَراضِعَ And We prevented him from suckling from the wet nurses مِنْ قَبْلُ before her following his trace فَقالَتْ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلى أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ يَكْفُلُونَهُ لَكُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُ ناصِحُونَ They will not fall short in his suckling and upbringing. (12) We had already forbidden him to suckle from nurses before that. She said, 'Shall I show you a household who will take care of him for you, and will be his well-wishers?'

And in al-Jawami it is narrated: When she said وَهُمْ لَهُ ناصِحُونَ ** (and will be his well-wishers), Haman said: "She surely knows him and knows his family." She said: "I only meant وَهُمْ ** (and they) are well-wishers ناصِحُونَ ** to the king (للملك)."

(١٣) فَرَدَدْناهُ إِلى أُمِّهِ كَيْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُها ** (13) So We restored him to his mother, that her eye might be comforted, with her son وَلا تَحْزَنَ ** and that she might not grieve, over his separation وَلِتَعْلَمَ أَنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقٌ ** and that she might know that Allah’s promise is true. a knowledge of witnessing (علم مشاهدة) وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** But most of them do not know. This story has already preceded in the narration of القمّيّ, detailed from الباقر (peace be upon him) in سورة طه, and he mentioned it in الإكمال in a simpler form than that.

(١٤) وَلَمَّا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ ** (14) And when he reached his maturity في المعاني عن الصادق عليه السلام : ثمان عشرة سنة وَاسْتَوى التحى آتَيْناهُ حُكْماً وَعِلْماً وَكَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [It means] eighteen years old, وَاسْتَوى (and became firm/settled) means his beard grew (التحى). We gave him judgment and knowledge, and thus do We reward the virtuous.**

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in the narrative that preceded, he said: Moses remained with Pharaoh in the most honored state until he reached the age of men. And [Pharaoh] used to disapprove of what Moses spoke concerning al-Tawhid (monotheism), until [Pharaoh] intended harm against him, so Moses departed from his presence.

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: And the Children of Israel used to seek and ask about him, but his news was concealed from them (فعمي عليهم خبره). Pharaoh was informed that they were seeking him and asking about him, so he sent [messengers] to them, increased their torment, separated them, and forbade them from giving news of him or asking about him. He said: So the Children of Israel went out on a moonlit night to an old man of theirs who possessed knowledge. They said: "We used to find comfort in narratives (الأحاديث), but how long shall we remain in this affliction?" He said: "By Allah, you shall not cease to be in it until Allah brings forth a youth from the progeny of Levi (لاوى) son of Jacob (يعقوب), whose name is Moses (موسى) son of Amran (عمران), a tall, curly-haired youth." While they were thus engaged, Moses approached, riding a mule, until he stopped before them. The old man raised his head and recognized him by the description. He asked him: "What is your name?" He replied: "Moses (موسى)." He asked: "Son of whom?" He replied: "Son of Amran (عمران)." The old man leaped toward him, took his hand, and kissed it. And they rushed to his feet and kissed them. So he recognized them, and they recognized him, and he took them as his followers (شيعته). He remained after that for as long as Allah willed, then he departed.

(١٥) وَدَخَلَ الْمَدِينَةَ ** (And he entered the city) A city from the cities of Pharaoh. This is according to al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him). عَلى حِينِ غَفْلَةٍ مِنْ أَهْلِها ** (when its people were unaware) They said that this was between the sunset (المغرب) and the evening (العشاء) prayers. فَوَجَدَ فِيها رَجُلَيْنِ يَقْتَتِلانِ هذا مِنْ شِيعَتِهِ وَهذا مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ ** (and found in it two men fighting: one of them was of his party and the other was of his enemy) One of them was among those who followed him in his religion, meaning from the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), and the other was from his opponents, meaning the Copts (القبط).

Al-Qummi in his previous narration said: One of them held the belief (بقول) of Moses, and the other held the belief (بقول) of Pharaoh. فَاسْتَغاثَهُ الَّذِي مِنْ شِيعَتِهِ عَلَى الَّذِي مِنْ عَدُوِّهِ ** (Thereupon the one who was of his party sought his help against the one who was of his enemy.) So he asked him to help him with assistance (الإعانة). For this reason, it was transitive using (على), and it was read (استعانه) [sought his assistance].

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "May the name bring you joy." He was asked: "What is the name?" He replied: "The Shi'ah (الشيعة)." Then He recited this verse. فَوَكَزَهُ مُوسى ** (So Moses struck him with his fist) So he struck the Copt (القبطي) with the gathered part of his palm (بجمع كفّه). فَقَضى عَلَيْهِ ** (and finished him off.) It was said that is, he killed him. Its origin is that he ended his life, derived from His saying: وقضينا إليه ذلك الأمر (And We decreed that matter to him).

In al-Uyun: Al-Rida (peace be upon him) was asked about this verse, given that the Prophets are infallible (معصومون). He replied: فَقَضى عَلَيْهِ (and finished him off) means against the enemy, by the decree (بحكم) of Allah, exalted be His remembrance. فَوَكَزَهُ ** (So Moses struck him with his fist) and he died. قالَ هذا مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطانِ إِنَّهُ عَدُوٌّ مُضِلٌّ مُبِينٌ ** (He said, 'This is of Satan's doing. He is indeed an enemy, misleading and manifest.') He (peace be upon him) said: It means the fighting (الاقتتال) that occurred between the two men, not what Moses did by killing him.

(١٦) قالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي ** (16) He said, 'My Lord! I have wronged myself.' He (peace be upon him) said: He means, "I placed myself in a place other than its proper place by entering this city." فَاغْفِرْ لِي So forgive me. He (peace be upon him) said: Meaning, "Cover me from Your enemies, lest they gain mastery over me and kill me." فَغَفَرَ لَهُ إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ. So He forgave him. Indeed, He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.**

(١٧) قالَ رَبِّ بِما أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَ ** (17) He said, 'My Lord! For the favour that You have bestowed upon me, He (peace be upon him) said: meaning, of the strength, until I killed a man with a single blow (بوكزة). فَلَنْ أَكُونَ ظَهِيراً لِلْمُجْرِمِينَ I will never be a supporter of the guilty.' He (peace be upon him) said: Rather, I will strive against them (أُجَاهِدُهُمْ) in Your path with this strength until You are pleased.

In al-Ikmal, in the previous narration, he said: And Moses (peace be upon him) had been granted an amplitude in body (بَسْطَة فِي الْجِسْم) and severity in striking (شِدَّة فِي الْبَطْش). He said: So the people mentioned it and his matter became widespread, and they said that Moses killed a man from Pharaoh's folk (آل فِرْعَوْن).

(١٨) فَأَصْبَحَ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ خائِفاً يَتَرَقَّبُ ** (18) So he woke up in the city, fearful and watchful. He watches out for what might happen (يترصد الاستفادة). فَإِذَا الَّذِي اسْتَنْصَرَهُ بِالْأَمْسِ يَسْتَصْرِخُهُ ** (18) Then behold, the one who had sought his help the day before was crying out to him for help. He seeks his aid against another (man). قالَ لَهُ مُوسى إِنَّكَ لَغَوِيٌّ مُبِينٌ Moses said to him, 'You are indeed a manifest stray.' Clear in misguidance (الغواية). In the narrative of al-Uyun, he said: He (Moses) said to him: "You fought a man **بِالْأَمْسِ ** (yesterday) and you fight today! I shall surely harm you." And he intended to strike him.

(١٩) فَلَمَّا أَنْ أَرادَ أَنْ يَبْطِشَ بِالَّذِي هُوَ عَدُوٌّ لَهُما ** (19) When he intended to strike him who was an enemy to both of them, (The enemy was) to Musa and the Israelite, because he (the enemy) was not upon their religion, and because the Copts (القبط) were enemies to the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل). قالَ يا مُوسى أَتُرِيدُ أَنْ تَقْتُلَنِي كَما قَتَلْتَ نَفْساً بِالْأَمْسِ إِنْ تُرِيدُ إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ جَبَّاراً فِي الْأَرْضِ ** he said, 'O Musa, do you intend to kill me as you killed a soul yesterday? You only desire to be a tyrant in the land, (meaning) one who acts arrogantly (متطاولاً) towards the people. وَما تُرِيدُ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُصْلِحِينَ ** and do not desire to be one of the reformers.' (meaning) among them. In a narration, it is said: قالَ ** (he said) refers to the one who was from his (Musa's) followers (شِيعته). I say: Perhaps the intent is that the Israelite said that, and it is as if when he (Musa) called him (the Israelite) misguided (غويّاً), he (the Israelite) thought that he (Musa) intended to strike him. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in his previous narration: When it was the next day, another man came and clung to that man who held the same view as Musa, so he sought Musa's help. When his companion looked at Musa, he said to him: **أَتُرِيدُ أَنْ تَقْتُلَنِي ** (Do you intend to kill me?). So, he (Musa) let go of his companion and fled.

** وَجاءَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَقْصَى الْمَدِينَةِ يَسْعى قالَ يا مُوسى إِنَّ الْمَلَأَ يَأْتَمِرُونَ بِكَ ** (20) And a man came running from the farthest end of the city. He said, 'O Moses, the elite are conspiring against you They are consulting concerning you (يَتَشاوَرُونَ بِسَبَبِكَ). And consultation (التشاور) was only named i'timār (ايتماراً) because each of the consultants commands (يأمر) the other and is commanded (يأتمر). ** لِيَقْتُلُوكَ فَاخْرُجْ إِنِّي لَكَ مِنَ النَّاصِحِينَ ** to kill you. So leave [the city]. I am one of your well-wishers.' It was said that he was the believer from the family of Pharaoh (مؤمن آل فرعون) and he was the cousin of Moses. And al-Qummi, in his previous narration, [said]: He was Pharaoh's treasurer (خازن فرعون), a believer in Moses, who had concealed his faith for six hundred years. He is the one concerning whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** وَقالَ رَجُلٌ مُؤْمِنٌ مِنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَكْتُمُ إيمانَهُ ** (28) And a faithful man from Pharaoh’s family, who concealed his faith, said, He said: The news of Moses killing the man reached Pharaoh, so he sought him to kill him. Thus, the believer sent [word] to Moses: ** إِنَّ الْمَلَأَ يَأْتَمِرُونَ بِكَ لِيَقْتُلُوكَ ** (the rest of) the Ayah.

(٢١) فَخَرَجَ مِنْها خائِفاً يَتَرَقَّبُ ** (21) Then he left the city, fearful and watchful. fearing the pursuit of a seeker قالَ رَبِّ نَجِّنِي مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** He said, 'My Lord! Deliver me from the wrongdoing lot.' Deliver me from them and protect me from their pursuit. Al-Qummi said in his previous narration: He was looking to his right and his left, and saying: رَبِّ نَجِّنِي مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ (My Lord! Deliver me from the wrongdoing lot). He said: And he passed towards مدين, and the distance between him and مدين was a journey of three days.

(٢٢) وَلَمَّا تَوَجَّهَ تِلْقاءَ مَدْيَنَ ** (22) When he turned his face toward Midian, opposite Midian, the village of Shu'ayb. It is said that it was named after Madyan ibn Ibrahim, and it was not under the authority (سلطان) of Pharaoh. قالَ عَسى رَبِّي أَنْ يَهْدِيَنِي سَواءَ السَّبِيلِ. he said, 'Maybe my Lord will guide me to the right path.' In al-Ikmal, in the previous narration: So he departed from Egypt without a mount, nor an animal, nor a servant. The earth protected him one time and raised him up another time, until he reached the land of Midian. He arrived at the base of a tree and descended, and behold, beneath it was a well.

(٢٣) وَلَمَّا وَرَدَ ماءَ مَدْيَنَ ** (23) When he arrived at the water of Midian, أي البئر meaning the well (البئر). وَجَدَ عَلَيْهِ أُمَّةً مِنَ النَّاسِ ** he found a crowd of people there جماعة كثيرة مختلفين يَسْقُونَ مواشيهم a large group (جماعة كثيرة) differing, watering their livestock. وَوَجَدَ مِنْ دُونِهِمُ ** and found two women keeping back [their flock] apart from them. في مكان أسفل من مكانهم امْرَأَتَيْنِ تَذُودانِ in a place lower than their place, two women keeping back (تَذُودانِ), تمنعان اغنامهما عن الماء لئلّا تختلط بأغنامهم preventing their sheep from the water so that they would not mix with the men's sheep. قالَ ما خَطْبُكُما ** He said, 'What is your business?' ما شأنكما تذودان What is your affair (شأنكما) that you are keeping back (تذودان)? قالَتا لا نَسْقِي حَتَّى يُصْدِرَ الرِّعاءُ ** They said, 'We do not water [our flock] until the shepherds have driven off [theirs]. يصرف الرعاة مواشيهم عنا لماء حذراً عن مزاحمة الرجال The shepherds drive their livestock away from the water from us, out of caution against jostling with the men. وقرء يصدر بفتح الياء وضمّ الدّال اي ينصرف And it was read (وقرء) يُصْدِرَ with the opening (فتح) of the yā (الياء) and the ḍamma (ضمّ) on the dāl (الدّال), meaning they depart (ينصرف). وَأَبُونا شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ ** And our father is an aged man.' كبير السنّ لا يستطيع ان يخرج للسّعي فيرسلنا اضطراراً. advanced in age (كبير السنّ), unable to go out for the effort (للسّعي), so He sends us out of necessity (اضطراراً).

**(٢٤) فَسَقى لَهُما ** (So he watered [their flock] for them) their livestock, out of mercy for them both.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) in his narration: When he reached ماءَ مَدْيَنَ ** (the waters of Midian), he saw a well from which people were drawing water for their sheep and beasts. So he sat down on one side, and he had not eaten anything for three days. Then he looked at two young women on one side, and with them were small flocks of sheep (غنيمات), and they were not approaching the well. So he said to them: "What is the matter with you that you are not drawing water?" They replied as Allah recounted. So Moses (موسى) took pity on them both and approached the well. He said to those at the well: "Draw a bucket for me, and a bucket for yourselves." The bucket (الدّلو) required ten men to pull it, but he drew a bucket alone for those at the well, and a bucket for the two daughters of Shu'ayb (شعيب), and he watered their sheep.

In al-Jawami (الجوامع) it is narrated: that the shepherds used to place a stone upon the mouth of the well which could not be lifted except by seven men, and it is said ten, and it is said forty, but he lifted it alone. He asked them for a bucket, and they gave him a bucket which could not be drawn except by ten men, but he drew water with it alone, a single time, and watered their sheep and sent them back (1).

ثُمَّ تَوَلَّى إِلَى الظِّلِ ** (Then he turned toward the shade) In al-Ikmal (الإكمال), in his narration: towards the tree, and he sat beneath it.

فَقالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي لِما أَنْزَلْتَ إِلَيَّ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَقِيرٌ (and said, 'My Lord! I am needy of whatever good You may send down to me.')

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) in his narration: And he was intensely hungry.

And in al-Kafi (الكافي) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He asked for food.

And in Nahj al-Balagha (نهج البلاغة): By Allah, he did not ask Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), for anything except bread to eat, because he was eating the herbs (بقلة) of the earth. The greenness of the herbs could be seen through the thinness (شفيف) of the lining (صفاق) of his stomach due to his emaciation (هزاله) and the wasting (تشذب) of his flesh.

And in al-Ikmal (الإكمال) it is narrated: that he said that while he was in need of half a date (شقّ تمرة).

** فَجاءَتْهُ إِحْداهُما تَمْشِي عَلَى اسْتِحْياءٍ قالَتْ إِنَّ أَبِي يَدْعُوكَ لِيَجْزِيَكَ ** (25) Then one of the two women came to him, walking bashfully. She said, 'My father invites you to pay you' ليكافيك أَجْرَ ما سَقَيْتَ لَنا جزاء سقيك لنا [to reward you] the recompense for what you watered for us, [as a reward] for your watering for us. Al-Qummi, in his narration (في حديثه), [said]: When the two daughters of Shu'ayb (peace be upon him) returned to Shu'ayb (peace be upon him), He said to them: "You have hastened the return (الرجوع)." So they informed Him of the story (بقصّة) of Moses (peace be upon him), though they did not know who He was. So Shu'ayb said to one of them: "Go to Him and invite Him so that we may reward Him the recompense (اجر) for what He watered for us." So she came to Him, as Allah related. Moses (peace be upon him) stood up and went with her, and she walked in front of Him. The winds blew upon her, revealing her lower body (عجزها). So Moses (peace be upon him) said to her: "Walk behind me (تأخّري) and guide me to the path by throwing a pebble (بحصاة) in front of me, which I will follow. For I am from a people who do not look at the backsides (ادبار) of women." فَلَمَّا جاءَهُ وَقَصَّ 'the wages for what you watered for us.' (25) When he came to him and recounted **عَلَيْهِ الْقَصَصَ قالَ لا تَخَفْ نَجَوْتَ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** 'the whole story to him, he said, 'Do not be afraid. You have been delivered from the wrongdoing lot.' He means Pharaoh and his people.

(26) ** قالَتْ إِحْداهُما يا أَبَتِ اسْتَأْجِرْهُ ** (One of the two said, 'Father, hire him) for tending the sheep ** إِنَّ خَيْرَ مَنِ اسْتَأْجَرْتَ الْقَوِيُّ الْأَمِينُ.** (The best one you can hire is the strong and trustworthy one.')

In al-Qummi (القمّيّ) in his narration: Shu'ayb said to her: "As for his strength, I have known it by the fact that he draws the bucket alone. So by what did you know his trustworthiness?" She said: "When he told me, 'Stay behind me and guide me to the path, for I am from a people who do not look at the backs of women,' I knew that he is not among those who look at the hindquarters of women. So this is his trustworthiness."

And in al-Faqih (الفقيه), narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: Shu'ayb said to her: "O my daughter, this one is strong; I have known it by his lifting the rock. And **الْأَمِينُ ** (the trustworthy one), from where did you know him?" She said: "O my father, I walked ahead of him, and he said, 'Walk behind me, and if I stray, guide me to the path, for I am from a people who do not look at the backs of women.'"

And in al-Majma' (المجمع) there is a narration close to this, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٢٧) قالَ إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُنْكِحَكَ إِحْدَى ابْنَتَيَّ هاتَيْنِ عَلى أَنْ تَأْجُرَنِي (١) (27) He said, 'I desire to marry you to one of these two daughters of mine, on condition that you hire yourself out to me ثَمانِيَ حِجَجٍ فَإِنْ أَتْمَمْتَ عَشْراً فَمِنْ عِنْدِكَ ** for eight years. But if you complete ten, it will be of your own accord.' So, its completion is from you as a favor (تفضّلاً), not as an obligation (الزاماً) upon you from me. وَما أُرِيدُ أَنْ أَشُقَّ عَلَيْكَ ** I do not want to put you to any hardship. by obligating the completion of the ten (years). سَتَجِدُنِي إِنْ شاءَ اللهُ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ ** If Allah wills, you will find me one of the righteous.' in good treatment (حسن المعاملة), gentleness (لين الجانب), and fulfilling the covenant (الوفاء بالمعاهدة).

(٢٨) قالَ ذلِكَ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكَ ** (28) He said, 'That is between me and you. We shall not deviate from it. ** أَيَّمَا الْأَجَلَيْنِ ** Whichever of the two terms the longer or the shorter of them ** قَضَيْتُ ** I fulfill, I will fulfill it for you. فَلا عُدْوانَ عَلَيَ there shall be no aggression against me. So do not transgress against me by demanding an increase. وَاللهُ عَلى ما نَقُولُ And Allah is witness to what we say.' regarding what we stipulate in the agreement (المشارطة), is a Trustee (وَكِيلٌ), a Witness (شاهد), a Guardian (حفيظ).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked which of ** الْأَجَلَيْنِ ** (the two terms) He fulfilled. He said: "The most complete and the longest of them." And in a narration: "If you are asked which of the two daughters He married, say the younger of them, and she is the one who came and said: ** يا أَبَتِ اسْتَأْجِرْهُ. ** (O my father! Hire him.)"

And from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked which of them was the one who ** قالَتْ إِنَّ أَبِي يَدْعُوكَ ** (said, 'My father invites you'). He said: "The one He married." He was asked: "And which of the two terms did He fulfill?" He said: "The most complete and the furthest of them: ten years." It was asked: "Did He consummate the marriage with her before the condition expired or after its completion?" He said: "Before it expired." It was asked: "If a man marries a woman and stipulates for her father a two-month hiring (اجارة), is that permissible (يجوز)?" He said: "Musa (موسى) knew that his condition would be fulfilled for Him." It was asked: "How?" He said: "He knew that He would remain until He fulfilled it."

And al-Qummi narrated from him عليه السلام: He said: "Marriage (النكاح) today in Islam is not permissible (يحلّ) through hiring (اجارة), such that one says: 'I will work for you for such and such a number of years on the condition that you marry me to your sister or your daughter.' He said: 'It is forbidden (حرام) because it is the price (ثمن) of her person (رقبتها), and she is more entitled to her dowry (مهرها).'" He said in al-Faqih, and in another hadith: "That was only permissible for Musa ibn 'Imran (موسى بن عمران) because He knew through revelation (الوحي) whether He would die before fulfilling the term or not, so He fulfilled the most complete of the two terms."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Yusha' ibn Nun (يوشع بن نون), the successor (وصّيُّ) of Musa (موسى), lived thirty years after Musa (موسى). Safra' bint Shu'ayb (صفراء بنت شعيب), the wife of Musa (موسى), rose up against him, saying: 'I am more entitled to the authority (الأمر) than you.' So he fought her, killed her combatants, and treated her captivity well."

footnote (١) Meaning: on the condition that you be a hired worker (*أجيراً*) for me for eight years.

(٢٩) ** فَلَمَّا قَضى مُوسَى الْأَجَلَ وَسارَ بِأَهْلِهِ ** meaning, with his wife, آنَسَ مِنْ جانِبِ الطُّورِ ناراً ** He saw from the direction that adjoins the Mount (الطور). (29) When Moses had fulfilled the term and was traveling with his family, he perceived a fire on the side of the Mount.

In al-Qummi's previous narration: He (Moses) said to Shu'ayb: "I must return to my homeland, my mother, and my family. What is due to me from you?" Shu'ayb (peace be upon him) said: "Whatever sheep my flocks give birth to this year that are spotted (بَلِقٍ) shall be yours." So, when Moses intended to send the ram among the ewes, he went to his staff (عصاه), peeled some parts of it and left others, and planted it in the middle of the sheep pen. He threw a spotted (أبلق) cloak over it, and then sent the ram among the ewes. The ewes did not give birth that year except to spotted (بَلِقاً) offspring. When the year had passed, Moses took his wife, and Shu'ayb provided him with provisions from his own stores, and Moses drove his sheep. When he intended to depart, he said to Shu'ayb: "I seek a staff (عصاً) to be with me." The staffs (عصيّ) of the Prophets were with him, having been inherited and collected in a house. Shu'ayb said to him: "Enter this house and take a staff from among the staffs." So he entered, and the staff (عصى) of Noah and Abraham sprang toward him and settled in his palm. He brought it out, and Shu'ayb looked at it and said: "Return it and take another." So he returned it to take another, but that very same staff sprang toward him again. He returned it, until he had done that three times. When Shu'ayb (peace be upon him) saw that, he said to him: "Go, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has singled you out for it." So he drove his sheep and departed, intending to go to Egypt (مصراً). When he reached a wilderness (مفازة) with his family, they were afflicted by severe cold, wind, and darkness, and the night covered them. Moses then looked at a fire that had appeared, as Allah the Almighty said:

فَلَمَّا قَضى مُوسَى الْأَجَلَ ** the Ayah. قالَ لِأَهْلِهِ امْكُثُوا إِنِّي آنَسْتُ ناراً** (29) When Moses had fulfilled the term and was traveling with his family, he perceived a fire on the side of the Mount. He said to his family, 'Wait, I perceive a fire.'

لَعَلِّي آتِيكُمْ مِنْها بِخَبَرٍ ** meaning, news of the path (بخبر الطريق). (29) I may bring you some news from it,

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): فَلَمَّا قَضى مُوسَى الْأَجَلَ وَسارَ بِأَهْلِهِ ** toward Jerusalem (بيت المقدس), he missed the path at night and saw a fire. قالَ لِأَهْلِهِ امْكُثُوا إِنِّي آنَسْتُ ناراً أَوْ جَذْوَةٍ ** meaning, a thick piece of wood (عود غليظ). It was recited with fathah (جَذْوَة) and ḍammah (جُذْوَة). مِنَ النَّارِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَصْطَلُونَ ** meaning, you may seek warmth (تستدفؤون بها) by it. (29) He said to his family, 'Wait, I perceive a fire, I may bring you some news from it, or a brand from the fire, so that you may warm yourselves.'

(٣٠) فَلَمَّا أَتاها نُودِيَ مِنْ شاطِئِ الْوادِ الْأَيْمَنِ ** (30) When he came to it, he was called from the right bank of the valley, قيل من الشاطئ الأيمن لموسى فِي الْبُقْعَةِ الْمُبارَكَةِ. It was said: from the right bank relative to Moses. in the blessed spot, في التهذيب عن الصادق عليه السلام : شاطِئِ الْوادِ الْأَيْمَنِ ** الذي ذكره الله تعالى في القرآن هو الفرات والْبُقْعَةِ الْمُبارَكَةِ هي كربلاء In al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The شاطِئِ الْوادِ الْأَيْمَنِ ** (right bank of the valley) which Allah the Almighty mentioned in the Quran is the Euphrates (الفرات), and the الْبُقْعَةِ الْمُبارَكَةِ (blessed spot) is Karbala (كربلاء). مِنَ الشَّجَرَةِ from the tree: قيل كانت نابتة على الشاطئ It was said: it was growing upon the bank. أَنْ يا مُوسى إِنِّي أَنَا اللهُ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ ** 'O Moses! Indeed, I am Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.' هذا وان خالف ما في طه والنمل لفظاً فلا يخالفه في المعنى. This, even if it differs in wording (لفظاً) from what is in [Surah] Ta Ha and al-Naml, does not differ from it in meaning (المعنى).

**(٣١) وَأَنْ أَلْقِ عَصاكَ ** (and cast down your staff.) meaning, so he cast it down, and it became a serpent and shook.

فَلَمَّا رَآها تَهْتَزُّ كَأَنَّها جَانٌ ** (But when he saw it quivering as if it were a serpent) a snake in form and size (الجثّة) or in speed (السرعة).

**وَلَّى مُدْبِراً ** (he turned his back) fleeing out of fear, وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ (and did not turn back) and did not return.

**يا مُوسى ** (O Moses!) He was called out to.

يا مُوسى أَقْبِلْ وَلا تَخَفْ إِنَّكَ مِنَ الْآمِنِينَ ** (‘O Moses! Come forward and do not fear. Indeed, you are one of the secure.) from dangers, for indeed, the Messengers do not fear in My presence (لَدَيَّ).

In al-Qummi's (القمّيّ) narration, which preceded, he said: So he approached the fire to take a brand, and behold, there was a tree and a fire blazing upon it. When he went toward the fire to take a brand from it, it lunged toward him, so he was terrified and ran, and the fire returned to the tree. He turned toward it, and it had returned to the tree. So he returned a second time to take a brand, and it lunged toward him, so he ran and left it. Then he turned, and it had returned to the tree. So he returned to it a third time, and it lunged toward him, so he ran وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ ** (and did not turn back), meaning, he did not return. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), called out to him:

أَنْ يا مُوسى إِنِّي أَنَا اللهُ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (‘O Moses! Indeed, I am Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.’) Moses said: "What is the proof of that?" Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), said: "What is in..."

بِيَمِينِكَ يا مُوسى قالَ هِيَ عَصايَ قالَ أَلْقِها يا مُوسى فَأَلْقاها فَإِذا هِيَ حَيَّةٌ تَسْعى ** (‘in your right hand, O Moses?’ He said, ‘It is my staff.’ He said, ‘Cast it down, O Moses!’ So he cast it down, and behold, it was a serpent moving swiftly.) So Moses was terrified by it and ran. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), called out to him: "Take it and do not fear. Indeed, you are one of the secure."

(٣٢)** اسْلُكْ يَدَكَ فِي جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضاءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوءٍ ** (32) Put your hand into your bosom, it will emerge white, without any defect.

He said: meaning, without defect (علّة). And that is because Musa was intensely dark-skinned (شديد السمرة), so he brought forth his hand from his bosom, and the world was illuminated for him.

**وَاضْمُمْ إِلَيْكَ جَناحَكَ مِنَ الرَّهْبِ ** And clasp your wings to your sides to allay the fear.

And it was recited (وقرئ) with a dammah on the rā’ (الرّاء) and with two fathahs. And perhaps that is for concealing the fear when facing the enemy, or for calming it, based on what is said, that fear is calmed by placing the hand upon the chest.

فَذانِكَ And it was recited (وقرئ) with the nūn intensified (بتشديد النون) بُرْهانانِ ** two arguments (حجّتان) مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** sent with them both (مرسلاً بهما) إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَائِهِ إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا قَوْماً ** a wicked (سوء), transgressing (فاسِقِينَ) people.

(٣٣) قالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي قَتَلْتُ مِنْهُمْ نَفْساً فَأَخافُ أَنْ يَقْتُلُونِ ** in retaliation for it (بها). (33) He said, 'My Lord, I killed one of them, and I fear that they will kill me [in retaliation].' (٣٤) وَأَخِي هارُونُ هُوَ أَفْصَحُ مِنِّي لِساناً فَأَرْسِلْهُ مَعِي رِدْءاً ** as a helper (معيناً). (34) And my brother Aaron is more eloquent than me in speech. So send him with me as a helper, And it was read (وقرء) without hamzah يُصَدِّقُنِي ** by summarizing the truth, establishing the proof (الحجّة), and exposing the falsehood of the doubt (الشبهة). And it was read (وقرء) in the jussive mood (مجزوماً): إِنِّي أَخافُ أَنْ يُكَذِّبُونِ (I fear that they will deny me), and my tongue does not obey me during argumentation (المحاجة).

(٣٥) قالَ سَنَشُدُّ عَضُدَكَ بِأَخِيكَ ** We will strengthen you with him وَنَجْعَلُ لَكُما سُلْطاناً ** dominance (غلبة) فَلا يَصِلُونَ إِلَيْكُما ** by overpowering (باستيلاء) بِآياتِنا أَنْتُما وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَكُمَا الْغالِبُونَ. (35) He said, 'We will strengthen your arm with your brother and invest you both with an authority so they will not reach you by Our signs, you two and those who follow you shall be the victors.' (٣٦) فَلَمَّا جاءَهُمْ مُوسى بِآياتِنا بَيِّناتٍ قالُوا ما هذا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُفْتَرىً وَما سَمِعْنا بِهذا فِي آبائِنَا الْأَوَّلِينَ. (36) So when Moses brought them Our manifest signs, they said, 'This is nothing but a fabricated magic, and we have not heard of this among our forefathers.' (٣٧) وَقالَ ** And it was read (وقرء) without the waw (بغير واو) مُوسى رَبِّي أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ جاءَ بِالْهُدى مِنْ عِنْدِهِ وَمَنْ تَكُونُ لَهُ عاقِبَةُ الدَّارِ ** the praiseworthy outcome (العاقبة المحمودة) for the abode of this world (لدار الدنيا), which is Paradise (الجنّة), because it was created as a passage (مجازاً) to the Hereafter (الآخرة). And تكون was read with the yā (بالياء) إِنَّهُ لا يُفْلِحُ الظَّالِمُونَ ** they will not attain success (لا يفوزون) through guidance (بالهدى) in this world (في الدنيا) nor a good outcome (وحسن العاقبة) in the Hereafter (في العقبى). (37) Moses said, 'My Lord knows best him who brings guidance from Him, and for whom will be the ultimate outcome of the [final] abode. Indeed, the wrongdoers will not be felicitous.'

(٣٨) وَقالَ فِرْعَوْنُ يا أَيُّهَا الْمَلَأُ ما عَلِمْتُ لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرِي ** (38) And Pharaoh said, 'O assembly! I do not know of any god for you other than me.' He negated his knowledge of a god other than himself, not the existence of one, as if he were doubtful about it. This is why he commanded the building of the tower (الصرح).

It is said in Tafsir al-Kalbi, narrated from Ibn Abbas, that Gabriel said to the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family): "O Muhammad, if you had seen me when Pharaoh was invoking the word of sincerity [when drowning]: آمَنْتُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُوا إِسْرائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (I believe that there is no god except Him in Whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am one of the submitters), and I was stuffing him into the water and mud due to the intensity of my anger towards him, fearing that he might repent, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, would accept his repentance." The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) said to him: "And what was the intensity of your anger towards him, O Gabriel?" He replied: "Because of his saying: أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلى ** (I am your Lord, the most high), and this was his last statement of the two. He only said it when he reached the sea. And his first statement was: ما عَلِمْتُ لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرِي ** (I do not know of any god for you other than me). There were forty years between the first and the last statement. فَأَوْقِدْ لِي يا هامانُ عَلَى الطِّينِ فَاجْعَلْ لِي صَرْحاً لَعَلِّي أَطَّلِعُ إِلى إِلهِ مُوسى وَإِنِّي لَأَظُنُّهُ مِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ** (So kindle a fire for me, O Haman, over the clay, and build me a tower, so that I may look up to Moses’ God, and indeed I think he is a liar)."

Al-Qummi, in his previous narration: So Haman built for him in the air صَرْحاً ** (a tower) until it reached a place in the air where a person could not stand due to the strong winds (الرياح القائمة) in the atmosphere. He said to Pharaoh: "We cannot increase beyond this." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent winds which threw it down. Thereupon, Pharaoh and Haman took a chest (التابوت) and proceeded to use four eagles (أنسر). They took their chicks and raised them until they gained strength and grew large. They then proceeded to the four sides of the chest, inserting a piece of wood into each side. They placed meat on the head of each piece of wood, starved the eagles, and tied their legs to the base of the wood. The eagles looked at the meat, swooped towards it, flapped their wings, and ascended with them into the air, flying throughout their day. Pharaoh said to Haman: "Look at the sky, have we reached it?" Haman looked and said: "I see the sky just as I used to see it from the earth, in the distance." Pharaoh said: "Look at the earth." He replied: "I do not see the earth, but I see the seas and the water." He said: The eagles continued to rise until the sun set, and the seas and water disappeared from their sight. Pharaoh said: "O Haman, look at the sky." He looked at the sky and said: "I see it just as I used to see it from the earth." When night enveloped them, Haman looked at the sky. Pharaoh asked: "Have we reached it?" He replied: "I see the stars just as I used to see them from the earth, and I see nothing of the earth except darkness." He said: Then the strong winds (الرياح القائمة) in the atmosphere changed direction, and the chest carrying them began to descend. It continued to fall with them until it landed on the earth. And Pharaoh was at his most arrogant (عتوّاً) at that time.

(٣٩) وَاسْتَكْبَرَ هُوَ وَجُنُودُهُ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِ ** (39) And he and his hosts acted arrogantly in the land unjustly without deserving it (الاستحقاق). The Almighty Allah said: "Arrogance (الكبرياء) is My cloak (ردائي) and Majesty (العظمة) is My lower garment (ازاري). Whoever contends with Me concerning one of them, I shall cast him into the Fire, and I do not care (ولا ابالي)." ** وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْنا لا يُرْجَعُونَ ** and they thought that they would not be returned to Us. by resurrection (النشور). And it was read with the opening of the yā' (الياء) and the breaking of the jīm (الجيم).

(40) فَأَخَذْناهُ وَجُنُودَهُ فَنَبَذْناهُمْ فِي الْيَمِ (40) So We seized him and his hosts, and cast them into the sea. as its explanation has passed. And in it is grandeur (فخامة) and magnification (تعظيم) of the significance of the seizing, and contempt (استحقار) for those who were seized, as if He seized them, despite their multitude, in a palm (of a hand) and cast them into the sea. فَانْظُرْ كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الظَّالِمِينَ. So observe how was the fate of the wrongdoers.

(٤١) وَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَئِمَّةً ** (41) And We made them imams (leaders) [They are] models of misguidance (قدوة ضلال). يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ وَيَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ لا يُنْصَرُونَ ** calling toward the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection they will not be helped. [Meaning:] by repelling the punishment (العذاب) from them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Verily, the Imams (الأئمّة) in the Book of Allah are two types of Imams (امامان). Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, said: وَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنا ** And We made them imams (leaders) guiding by Our command. [Meaning:] not by the command of the people. They prioritize the command of Allah before their own command, and the ruling (حكم) of Allah before their own ruling (حكم). He said: وَجَعَلْناهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ ** And We made them imams (leaders) calling toward the Fire. [Meaning:] They prioritize their command before the command of Allah, and their ruling (حكم) before the ruling (حكم) of Allah, and they follow their desires (أهوائهم), contrary to what is in the Book of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ).

(٤٢) وَأَتْبَعْناهُمْ فِي هذِهِ الدُّنْيا لَعْنَةً expulsion (طرداً) from mercy (الرحمة) وَيَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ هُمْ مِنَ الْمَقْبُوحِينَ from those whose faces have been made ugly (قبّح وجوههم). (42) And We pursued them with a curse in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection they will be among the repulsive. (٤٣) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ the Torah (التوراة) مِنْ بَعْدِ ما أَهْلَكْنَا الْقُرُونَ الْأُولى the peoples (أقوام) of Noah (نوح), Hud (هود), Salih (صالح), and Lot (لوط). (43) Certainly We gave Moses the Book after We had destroyed the former generations,

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: Allah has not destroyed a people (قوماً), nor a generation (قرناً), nor a nation (أمّة), nor the inhabitants of a town (اهل قرية) with a punishment from the heaven since He sent down the Torah (التوراة) upon the face of the earth, except for the inhabitants of the town who were transformed (مسخوا) into apes (قردة). Have you not seen that Allah the Almighty said: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ ** (the Ayah) بَصائِرَ لِلنَّاسِ وَهُدىً وَرَحْمَةً لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ.** (43) Certainly We gave Moses the Book after We had destroyed the former generations, as eye-openers for mankind, and a guidance and a mercy, so that they may take admonition.

(٤٤) وَما كُنْتَ بِجانِبِ الْغَرْبِيِ ** (44) And you were not on the western side [You were not] on the side of the western Mount Tur, where Allah spoke to Moses therein, إِذْ قَضَيْنا (when We decreed), meaning: We revealed (أوحينا), إِلى مُوسَى الْأَمْرَ ** and We spoke to him, وَما كُنْتَ مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ ** to His speaking to him. (45) وَلكِنَّا أَنْشَأْنا قُرُوناً فَتَطاوَلَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْعُمُرُ ** (45) But We brought forth generations, and ages passed them by. So the reports were distorted, the laws (الشرايع) were changed, and the sciences (العلوم) were obliterated, so We revealed to you. وَما كُنْتَ ثاوِياً ** [You were not] residing (مقيماً) فِي أَهْلِ مَدْيَنَ ** and they are Shu'ayb and those who believed in him, تَتْلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ ** It is said (قيل): meaning, so you recite to the people of Mecca آياتِنا ** which contain their story, وَلكِنَّا كُنَّا مُرْسِلِينَ ايّاك ومخبرين لك بها. (but We were sending you and informing you of it).

(46) ** وَما كُنْتَ بِجانِبِ الطُّورِ إِذْ نادَيْنا وَلكِنْ رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ وَلكِنْ ** (46) You were not on the side of the Mount when We called out, but it is a mercy from your Lord, and yet

We taught you, as a mercy, so that you may warn a people to whom no warner came before you, due to their falling into a period (فترة) between you and those prophets who preceded you, **لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ ** (that they may take admonition).

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent Moses son of Imran, chose him as an intimate confidant (نجيّا), split the sea for him, saved the Children of Israel, and gave him the Torah and the Tablets, he saw his standing with his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic. So he said: "O Lord, You have honored me with an honor You have not bestowed upon anyone before me." Allah, Glorious is His Majesty, said: "O Moses, do you not know that Muhammad is superior in My sight to all My angels and all My creation?" Moses said: "O Lord, if Muhammad is more honored in Your sight than all Your creation, then is there anyone among the families (آل) of the prophets more honored than my family (آلي)?" Allah, Glorious is His Majesty, said: "O Moses, do you not know that the superiority of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) over all the families of the prophets is like the superiority of Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) over all the Messengers?" Moses said: "O Lord, if the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) are thus, then is there any nation (أمّة) among the nations of the prophets superior in Your sight to my nation, over whom I shaded the clouds, and upon whom I sent down the manna (المنّ) and the quails (السلوى), and for whom I split the sea?" Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "O Moses, do you not know that the superiority of the Nation of Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) over all nations is like His superiority over all My creation?" Moses (عليه السلام) said: "O Lord, I wish I could see them." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him: "O Moses, you will not see them, and this is not the time of their appearance. But you will soon see them in the Gardens (الجنان), the Gardens of Eden (جنّات عدن) and al-Firdaws, in the presence of Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), reveling in its bliss and rejoicing with its maidens (حيرانها). Do you wish that I make you hear their speech?" He said: "Yes, my God." Allah, Glorious is His Majesty, said: "Stand before Me and tighten your waist-wrapper (ميزرك), standing like the humble servant before the Majestic King." Moses did so. Then our Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, called out: "O Nation of Muhammad!" And they all responded, while they were still in the loins (أصلاب) of their fathers and the wombs (أرحام) of their mothers: "Here I am, O Allah, here I am (لبّيك اللهمّ لبّيك). There is no partner for You, here I am (لبّيك). Indeed, all praise, grace (النعمة), and sovereignty (الملك) belong to You. There is no partner for You." He said: So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made that response the slogan (شعاراً) for the pilgrim (لحاج). Then our Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, called out: "O Nation of Muhammad! Indeed, My decree concerning you is that My Mercy has preceded My Wrath, and My Pardon is before My Punishment. I have responded to you before you called Me, and I have given you before you asked Me. Whoever meets Me bearing witness that there is no god except Allah, alone, without any partner, and that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger, truthful in his sayings and rightful in his deeds, and that Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) is his brother, his successor (وصيّه) after him, and his guardian (وليّه), and commits to his obedience just as he commits to the obedience of Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), and that his chosen, pure, purified, wondrously rewarded (المثابين العجائب) guardians (أوليائه) are the signs (آيات) of Allah and the proofs (حجج) of Allah's evidences after them both—those are His guardians (أوليائه)—I shall admit him into My Paradise, even if his sins are like the foam of the sea." He said: Then, when Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), He said: "O Muhammad, وَما كُنْتَ بِجانِبِ الطُّورِ إِذْ نادَيْنا ** (You were not on the side of the Mount when We called out) your nation with this honor (الكرامة)." Then the Mighty and Majestic said to Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): "Say: الحمد لِلَّه ربِّ العالمين (All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds) for what He has singled me out with of this virtue (الفضيلة)." And He said to his nation: "Say: الحمد لِلّهِ ربِّ العالمين (All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds) for what He has singled us out with of these virtues (الفضائل)."

** وَلَوْ لا أَنْ تُصِيبَهُمْ مُصِيبَةٌ بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُوا رَبَّنا لَوْ لا أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنا رَسُولاً فَنَتَّبِعَ آياتِكَ وَنَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (Were it not that a disaster should befall them because of what their hands have sent ahead, and they should say, 'Our Lord, why did You not send us an apostle, so that we might follow Your signs and be among the faithful?')

Its answer (جوابه) is omitted, meaning, were it not for their saying, when punishment afflicts them because of their disbelief and their acts of disobedience: رَبَّنا ** (Our Lord), why did You not ** أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنا رَسُولاً ** (send us a messenger) who would convey to us ** آياتِكَ ** (Your signs), so we might follow them and be among the affirmers (المصدّقين). [We would not have sent you]. Meaning, We only sent you to cut off their excuse and to establish the proof (الحجّة) against them.

(٤٨) فَلَمَّا جاءَهُمُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ عِنْدِنا قالُوا لَوْ لا أُوتِيَ مِثْلَ ما أُوتِيَ مُوسى ** (48) So when the truth came to them from Us, they said, 'Why was he not given the like of what Moses was given?' (This refers to) the Book (الكتاب) as a whole, and the hand (اليد), and the staff (العصا), and other things, (said) as a suggestion and out of obstinacy (تعنّتاً). أَوَلَمْ يَكْفُرُوا بِما أُوتِيَ مُوسى مِنْ قَبْلُ ** Did they not disbelieve in what Moses was given before? Meaning their kinsmen (أبناء جنسهم) in opinion (الرّاي) and doctrine (المذهب), who were the disbelievers (كفرة) of Moses' time. قالُوا سِحْرانِ ** They said, 'Two sorceries,' It was said that this means Moses (peace be upon him) and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And al-Qummi said (it means) Moses and Aaron. And it was recited (وقرء) as سحران (two sorceries) for emphasis (مبالغة). Or they mean by them (بهما) the Torah (التوراة) and the Qur'an (القرآن), (said) in mutual support (تظاهراً), meaning cooperation (تعاوناً) due to the agreement (توافق) of the two Books (الكتابين), or due to the manifestation of those miracles (الخوارق). وَقالُوا إِنَّا بِكُلٍ ** and they said, 'Indeed, we disbelieve in both.' In both of them, or in every one of the Prophets (الأنبياء), (we are) disbelievers (كافِرُونَ).

(٤٩) قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِكِتابٍ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ هُوَ أَهْدى مِنْهُما ** (Say, 'Then bring a Book from Allah which is a better guide than the two [Books], so that I may follow it, should you be truthful.') [Referring to] what was revealed to Moses and upon me [Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم], أَتَّبِعْهُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ.**

(٥٠) فَإِنْ لَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَكَ فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّما يَتَّبِعُونَ أَهْواءَهُمْ ** (But if they do not respond to you, know that they only follow their desires.) Since if they followed a proof (حُجَّة), they would have brought it. وَمَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَواهُ ** (And who is more astray than him who follows his desires) is an interrogative [statement] meaning negation. بِغَيْرِ هُدىً مِنَ اللهِ. (without any guidance from Allah?)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "It means he who takes his opinion (رَأْيَهُ) as his religion without an Imam from the Imams of Guidance (أَئِمَّة الهُدى)." In al-Basair, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The like of it.

إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot.) [Meaning] those who wronged themselves by plunging into the pursuit of desire (الهوى).

(٥١) وَلَقَدْ وَصَّلْنا لَهُمُ الْقَوْلَ ** (Certainly We have brought the Word to them continuously, so that they may take admonition.) We made some of it follow others in the revelation, so that the reminder (التذكير) may be continuous, or in the arrangement (النظم), so that the call (الدعوة) may be established by the proof (الحجّة), and the admonitions (المواعظ) by the promises (المواعيد), and the counsels (النصايح) by the lessons (العبر).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Imam to Imam (امام الى امام). And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Imam after Imam (امامٍ بعد امام) **لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ ** (so that they may take admonition) and thus obey.

(٥٢)الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ هُمْ بِهِ يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (52) Those whom We gave the Book before him believe in it. It is said that this was revealed concerning the believers among the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب). (٥٣) وَإِذا يُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ قالُوا آمَنَّا بِهِ ** (53) And when it is recited to them, they say, 'We believe in it. meaning, that it is the speech of Allah. إِنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّنا إِنَّا كُنَّا مِنْ قَبْلِهِ مُسْلِمِينَ ** It is the truth from our Lord. Indeed, we were Muslims before it.' when they saw its mention in the preceding Books. (٥٤) أُولئِكَ يُؤْتَوْنَ أَجْرَهُمْ مَرَّتَيْنِ بِما صَبَرُوا وَيَدْرَؤُنَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ السَّيِّئَةَ (54) These will be given their reward twice, because they were patient, and they repel evil with good. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: بِما صَبَرُوا ** (because they were patient) means upon dissimulation (التقية). And He said: بِالْحَسَنَةِ ** (with good) means dissimulation (التقية), and السَّيِّئَةَ ** (evil) means divulging secrets (الإذاعة). And al-Qummi said: They are the Imams (peace be upon them). He said: And al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "We are patient ones (صُبَّر), and our followers are more patient than us. That is because we were patient concerning what we know, and صَبَرُوا ** (they were patient) concerning what they do not know." He said: And His saying يَدْرَؤُنَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ السَّيِّئَةَ ** (and they repel evil with good) means they repel the evil deed of one who wrongs them with their good deeds. And it is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: Follow بِالْحَسَنَةِ السَّيِّئَةَ ** (evil with good) [so that] it wipes it out. وَمِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ يُنْفِقُونَ ** and spend out of what We have provided them. in the way of goodness.

(٥٥) وَإِذا سَمِعُوا اللَّغْوَ أَعْرَضُوا عَنْهُ ** (55) When they hear frivolous talk, they turn away from it out of nobility (تكرّماً). Al-Qummi said: Al-Laghw (اللَّغْوَ) is lying (الكذب), amusement (اللهو), and singing (الغناء). He said: And they are the Imams (peace be upon them), turning away from all of that. And they said to the frivolous ones (اللّاغين): لَنا أَعْمالُنا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمالُكُمْ سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ** and say, 'Our deeds belong to us, and your deeds belong to you. Peace be to you! abandoning them (متاركة لهم) and bidding them farewell (وتوديعاً). لا نَبْتَغِي الْجاهِلِينَ ** We do not seek the ignorant.' We do not seek their company (صحبتهم) nor do we desire it.

(56) ** إِنَّكَ لا تَهْدِي مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ وَلكِنَّ اللهَ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ** (Indeed, you cannot guide whomever you wish, but Allah guides whomever He wishes, and He knows best the guided ones.)

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Abu Talib. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to say: "O uncle, say لا إِله إلّا الله (There is no god except Allah), so that I may intercede for you with it on the Day of Resurrection." So he would say: "O son of my brother, I know myself better." When he died, Al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib testified before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he had spoken it at the time of death. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "As for me, I did not hear it from him, but I hope that I will intercede for him on the Day of Resurrection." And He said: "If I were to stand at the Praiseworthy Station (المقام المحمود), I would intercede for my mother, my father, my uncle, and a brother who was my sworn ally (مواخياً) during the Jahiliyyah (Age of Ignorance)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The likeness of Abu Talib is like the People of the Cave (أصحاب الكهف); they concealed faith (الإيمان) and manifested polytheism (الشرك), so Allah gave them أَجْرَهُمْ مَرَّتَيْنِ. (their reward twice over.)

I say: He only concealed faith (الإيمان) and manifested polytheism (الشرك) so that he would be more capable of supporting the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, as is understood from other narrations (أخبار أخر).

And narrated from him عليه السلام: It was said to him: "They claim that Abu Talib عليه السلام was an unbeliever (كافراً)." He replied: "They lied. How could he be an unbeliever when he said: 'Do you not know that we found Muhammad a Prophet like Moses, inscribed (خطّ) in the first of the Books (أوّل الكتب)?'" And in another hadith: "How could Abu Talib be an unbeliever when he said: 'They certainly knew that our son does not lie:

In our presence, nor does he heed the saying of falsehoods (الأباطل)

And a white one (أبيض) by whose face the clouds are asked for rain

The refuge (ثمال) of the orphans, the protection (عصمة) for the widows (الأرامل).'"

I say: "Inscribed (خطّ) in the first of the Books (أوّل الكتب)" means that this ruling is affirmed in the First Book, meaning the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ). And al-Abyad (الأبيض) (the white one) is the man of pure honor (النقيّ العرض). And al-Thimal (الثمال) (the refuge), like the word al-Ghiyath (الغياث), is the one who manages the affairs of his people. And al-Armalah (الأرملة) is a woman among women who has no husband.

And narrated from Imam al-Kazim عليه السلام: He was asked: "Was the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم subject to the authority (محجوجاً) of Abu Talib?" He said: "No, rather he was entrusted with the bequests (الوصايا), and he handed them over to him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." It was asked: "Did he hand over the bequests to him on the basis that he was subject to his authority?" He said: "If he had been subject to his authority, he would not have handed over the bequest (الوصيّة) to him." It was asked: "What was the state (حال) of Abu Talib?" He said: "He affirmed the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and what He brought, and he handed over the bequests to Him, and died that very day."

I say: The meaning of "subject to the authority (محجوجاً) of Abu Talib" is that Abu Talib was a proof (حجّة) over him before He was sent (as a Prophet). And by al-Wasaya (الوصايا) (the bequests) is meant the bequests of the Prophets عليهم السلام. "On the basis that he was subject to his authority (محجوج به)" means on the basis that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was a proof (حجّة) over him. And His saying, "he would not have handed over the bequest (الوصيّة) to him," means that the bequest only transfers from the one who has precedence (التقدّم).

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: When Abu Talib passed away, Gabriel (جبرئيل) descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, depart from Mecca, for you have no helper (ناصر) here." And Quraysh rose up against the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so He departed fleeing until He came to a mountain in Mecca called al-Hajun (الحجون), and He went to it.

And narrated from him عليه السلام, he said: Gabriel عليه السلام descended upon the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, your Lord sends greetings to you and says: 'I have forbidden the Fire upon the loins (صلب) that brought you down, the womb (بطن) that carried you, and the lap (حجر) that sheltered you.' The loins are the loins of your father, Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib. The womb that carried you is Aminah bint Wahb. As for the lap that sheltered you, it is the lap of Abu Talib." And in one narration, he added: "And Fatimah bint Asad."

And in Bisharat al-Mustafa, narrated from him, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful عليهم السلام, he said: One day, he was sitting in al-Rahbah (الرّحبة) and the people were gathered. A man stood up and said to him: "O Commander of the Faithful, you are in the station (المكان) to which Allah has brought you, yet your father is being punished in the Fire." He said to him: "Silence! May Allah break your mouth (فضّ الله فاك)! By Him Who sent Muhammad as a Prophet with the truth, if my father were to intercede for every sinner on the face of the earth, Allah Almighty would grant him intercession for them! How can my father be punished in the Fire while his son is the distributor (قسيم) of the Fire?" Then he said: "By Him Who sent Muhammad with the truth, the light (نور) of Abu Talib on the Day of Resurrection will extinguish the lights of creation, except for five lights: the light of Muhammad, my light, the light of Fatimah, and the lights of Al-Hasan and Al-Husayn, and the Imams عليهم السلام who are descended from them, because his light is from our light, which Allah Almighty created two thousand years before the creation of Adam."

And in al-Majma': We mentioned in Surat al-An'am that the People of the House (أهل البيت) عليهم السلام have unanimously agreed that Abu Talib died as a Muslim (مسلماً), and the narrations (الرّوايات) from them عليهم السلام confirming this are abundant. We cited there a portion of his poetry (اشعاره) which indicates his affirmation (تصديقه) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his belief in Divine Unity (توحيده). Indeed, fully enumerating all of it would not be contained by the scrolls (الطوامير). What has been narrated concerning this in the books of al-Maghazi (المغازي) and others is too numerous to count, revealing therein how he confronted those who confronted the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, defended Him, and affirmed His prophethood. Some reliable sources (الثقات) have said that his odes (قصائده) concerning this meaning—which dispel the knots of magic (عقد السّحر) and cast dust in the face of time (تغبر في وجه الدّهر)—amount to the volume of a single book (مجلّد) or more. And there is no doubt that he did not choose to fully manifest his opposition to the enemies, seeking to reconcile them and employing good management (حسن تدبير) in repelling their plots, lest they compel the Messenger to what they compelled Him to after his death.

(٥٧) ** وَقالُوا إِنْ نَتَّبِعِ الْهُدى مَعَكَ نُتَخَطَّفْ مِنْ أَرْضِنا ** (57) They say, 'Should we follow the guidance with you, we will be snatched away from our land.' We will be expelled from it.

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Quraysh when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله called them to Islam and the emigration (الهجرة). Ibn Tawus narrated it from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام.

And in Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from al-Sajjad عليه السلام, that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "By Him in Whose hand is my soul, I shall surely call the white and the black to this matter, and those who are upon the peaks of the mountains and the depths of the seas. And I shall surely call Persia (فارس) and Rome (الرّوم) to it." So, Quraysh became arrogant (جبرت) and haughty (استكبرت), and they said to Abu Talib: "Do you not hear what your nephew is saying? By Allah, if Persia (فارس) and Rome (الرّوم) were to hear this, they would surely snatch us away from **أَرْضِنا ** (our land) and they would uproot the Ka'bah stone by stone." So, Allah the Almighty revealed this Ayah:

** أَوَلَمْ نُمَكِّنْ لَهُمْ حَرَماً آمِناً ** Did We not establish for them a secure sanctuary

Did We not make their place a sanctuary (حَرَماً) possessing security due to the sanctity of the House (البيت)?

** يُجْبى إِلَيْهِ ** to which are brought

It is carried to it and gathered therein. And it was recited (وقرء) with the tā' (بالتّاء).

** ثَمَراتُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** the fruits of all things,

from every direction (أوب).

** رِزْقاً مِنْ لَدُنَّا ** as a provision from Us?

If this is their condition while they are worshippers of idols (عبدة الأصنام), then how could We expose them to fear and being snatched away (للتخطّف) if they were monotheists (موحّدين)?

** وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** But most of them do not know.

They are ignorant (جهلة); they do not perceive it (لا يتفطّنون له).

(٥٨)** وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنا مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ بَطِرَتْ مَعِيشَتَها ** (How many a town We destroyed whose inhabitants exulted in their luxury)

And how many people of a town whose condition was like your condition in security (الامن) and ease of life (خفض العيش) until they became insolent (أشروا), so Allah destroyed them with it and ruined their homes (ديارهم). فَتِلْكَ مَساكِنُهُمْ ** desolate (خاوية) لَمْ تُسْكَنْ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** due to the ill-omen (شوم) of their acts of disobedience (معاصيهم). وَكُنَّا نَحْنُ الْوارِثِينَ. (and We have been the inheritors.)

(٥٩) وَما كانَ رَبُّكَ And His custom was not مُهْلِكَ الْقُرى حَتَّى يَبْعَثَ فِي أُمِّها destroy the towns until He had sent to their mother (59) Your Lord would never destroy the towns until He had sent to their mother in its origin (أصلها), because its people are more discerning (أفطن) and nobler (أنبل). رَسُولاً يَتْلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ آياتِنا a messenger reciting Our signs to them. a messenger reciting Our signs to them. in order to establish the proof (الحجّة) and cut off the excuse (المعذرة). وَما كُنَّا مُهْلِكِي الْقُرى إِلَّا وَأَهْلُها ظالِمُونَ Nor would We destroy the towns unless their inhabitants were wrongdoers. by denying the Messengers (الرّسل) and persisting (العتوّ) in disbelief (الكفر).

(٦٠) ** وَما أُوتِيتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَمَتاعُ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَزِينَتُها ** (And whatever you have been given is the provision of the life of the world and its glitter.) You enjoy and adorn yourselves with it during the period of your passing life. ** وَما عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (and what is with Allah) And that is His reward, which is better than that, because it is pure pleasure and complete joy. ** وَأَبْقى ** (is better and more lasting.) because it is eternal (أبديّ). ** أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ أَ تَسْتَبْدِلُونَ الَّذِي هُوَ أَدْنى بِالَّذِي هُوَ خَيْرٌ ** (Do you not exercise your reason? Will you exchange what is inferior for what is better?) And it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتاء).

(٦١) أَفَمَنْ وَعَدْناهُ وَعْداً حَسَناً فَهُوَ لاقِيهِ كَمَنْ مَتَّعْناهُ مَتاعَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (61) Is he whom We have promised a good promise, which he will receive, like him whom We have provided with the provision of the life of the world? and its adornment, which is tainted with pains, disturbed by hardships, and followed by sorrow over its cessation, ثُمَّ هُوَ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ مِنَ الْمُحْضَرِينَ ** (Then he, on the Day of Resurrection, will be among those brought forth) for the reckoning or the punishment. And this Ayah is like the conclusion (النتيجة) for the one preceding it.

(٦٢) وَيَوْمَ يُنادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكائِيَ الَّذِينَ كُنْتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ ** (62) The day He calls out to them and says, 'Where are My partners whom you used to claim?' you claim them as My partners. (٦٣) قالَ الَّذِينَ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلُ ** (63) Those against whom the sentence has been sealed will say, meaning His saying: لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ ** (I will surely fill hell with jinn and humans, all together) and other verses of warning (الوعيد*). رَبَّنا هؤُلاءِ الَّذِينَ أَغْوَيْنا ** 'Our Lord, these are the ones whom we led astray. meaning, these are the ones whom we led astray. أَغْوَيْناهُمْ كَما غَوَيْنا تَبَرَّأْنا إِلَيْكَ ** We led them astray just as we went astray. We disavow ourselves before You. from them and from the disbelief (الكفر) they chose. ما كانُوا إِيَّانا يَعْبُدُونَ ** They did not worship us.' And rather, they worship their desires (أهواءهم).

(٦٤) وَقِيلَ ادْعُوا شُرَكاءَكُمْ فَدَعَوْهُمْ (64) And it will be said, 'Invoke your partners.' So they will invoke them, due to extreme confusion (الحيرة). فَلَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَهُمْ ** but they will not respond to them. because of their inability (عجزهم) to respond (الإجابة) or provide help (النصرة). وَرَأَوُا الْعَذابَ لَوْ أَنَّهُمْ كانُوا يَهْتَدُونَ (If only they had been guided) to a means (وجه من الحيل) by which they could repel the punishment, or لَوْ ** is for wishing (للتمنّي), meaning: they wished (تمنّوا) that they had been guided (مهتدين).

(٦٥) وَيَوْمَ يُنادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ ما ذا أَجَبْتُمُ الْمُرْسَلِينَ (65) The day He calls out to them and says, 'What answer did you give to the apostles?' (٦٦) فَعَمِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَنْباءُ يَوْمَئِذٍ (66) On that day the news will be obscured for them, [The news] does not guide them. Its original form (أَصْلُهُ) was "so they were blinded (فَعَمُوا) from the news (الْأَنْبَاء)," but it was inverted (عُكِسَ) for emphasis (مُبَالَغَة) and to indicate that what is present in the mind (الذّهن) only comes to it from the outside (مِنْ خَارِج). Thus, if it is missed (أَخْطَأَ), they have no means (حِيلَة) to recall it (اِسْتِحْضَارُهُ). **فَهُمْ لا يَتَساءَلُونَ ** so they will not question one another. They do not ask one another concerning the answer.

(٦٧)** فَأَمَّا مَنْ تابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً فَعَسى أَنْ يَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُفْلِحِينَ ** (67) But as for him who repents, believes and acts righteously, maybe he will be among the felicitous.

The word عسى (maybe) here signifies certainty (تحقيق), following the custom of the Generous Ones (عادة الكرام), or it is for hope (ترجّى) on the part of the penitent (التائب), meaning, let him expect that he will succeed (يُفْلِح).

Al-Qummi said that the general populace (العامّة) have narrated that this means the call (النداء) on the Day of Resurrection (القيامة). But as for the specific followers (الخاصّة):

Narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: When the servant enters his grave and is terrified by it, he is questioned about the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And it is said to him: "What do you say about this man who was among you?" If he is a believer (مؤمناً), he says: "I bear witness that he is the Messenger of Allah, who came with the truth (بالحقّ)." It is said to him: "Sleep a sleep in which there is no dream (لا حلم فيها)." And the Devil (الشيطان) withdraws from him, and his grave is widened for him by seven cubits (سبعة أذرع), and he sees his place in Paradise (الجنّة). But if he is a disbeliever (كافراً), he says: "I do not know (ما أدري)." He is struck a blow that is heard by everyone Allah has created, except for man (الإنسان). And the Devil (الشيطان) is given power over him, and he has two eyes of copper (نحاس) or fire (نار) that flash like lightning (كالبرق الخاطف). And he says to him: "I am your brother." And snakes (الحيّات) and scorpions (العقارب) are given power over him, and his grave becomes dark upon him. Then it squeezes him with a squeeze that causes his ribs (أضلاعه) to interlace (يختلف). Then he (peace be upon him) demonstrated with his fingers, interlacing them (فشرجها).

(٦٨) وَرَبُّكَ يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ وَيَخْتارُ ما كانَ لَهُمُ الْخِيَرَةُ ** (68) Your Lord creates whatever He wishes and chooses; they have no choice.**

Meaning: the choosing (التخيّر), like al-tīrah (الطّيرة) meaning al-taṭayyur (التّطير) [omen-taking]. That is, none of His creation has the right to choose over Him, or none has the right to choose anything except by His power, His will, and His choice. Glory be to Allah (سُبْحانَ اللهِ), a declaration of His transcendence, that anyone should dispute with Him or compete with His choice. وَتَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** (and highly exalted is He above what they associate [with Him])—meaning, above their association (اشراكهم).

Al-Qummi said: **يَخْتارُ ** (chooses)—Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, chooses the Imam, and they do not have the right to choose.

In al-Kafi and al-Majalis, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a hadith concerning the virtue and description of the Imam, He said: "Do you know the status of the Imamate and its position among the Ummah, such that their choice (اختيارهم) is permissible in it?" Until He said: "They have attempted a difficult thing, spoken a lie (افكاً), gone far astray, and fallen into confusion when they abandoned the Imam despite having insight (بصيرة). Satan adorned their deeds for them, so he barred them from the path, and they were not insightful. They turned away from the choice of Allah and the choice of the Messenger of Allah to their own choice, while the Quran calls out to them: وَرَبُّكَ يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ وَيَخْتارُ ما كانَ لَهُمُ الْخِيَرَةُ سُبْحانَ اللهِ وَتَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** (Your Lord creates whatever He wishes and chooses; they have no choice. Glory be to Allah and highly exalted is He above what they associate [with Him]). And He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَما كانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ وَلا مُؤْمِنَةٍ إِذا قَضَى اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَمْراً أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُمُ الْخِيَرَةُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِمْ.** (A faithful man or woman may not have any choice in the matter, when Allah and His Apostle have decided on a matter.)"

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Qa'im (peace be upon him): He was asked about the reason (العلة) that prevents the people from choosing the Imam for themselves. He said: "Is he a reformer (مصلح) or a corrupter (مفسد)?" It was said: "A reformer." He said: "Is it permissible that their choice should fall upon the corrupter, given that no one knows what crosses the mind of another, whether it be righteousness (صلاح) or corruption (فساد)?" It was said: "Yes." He said: "That is the reason (العلّة). I shall present it to you with a proof (برهان) that your intellect will submit to."
