Then He (peace be upon him) said: "Inform me about the Messengers whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, chose, upon whom He sent down the Book, and whom He supported with revelation (الوحي) and infallibility (العصمة). Since they are the standards (اعلام) of the nations, are they more guided in choosing than others, such as Moses and Jesus? Is it permissible, despite the abundance of their intellect, that when they choose, their choice should fall upon a hypocrite (المنافق) while they believe him to be a believer?" It was said: "No." He said: "This is Moses, the Speaker of Allah (كليم الله), despite the abundance of his intellect, the perfection of his knowledge, and the descent of revelation upon him, he chose seventy men from the notables of his people and the leaders of his army for the appointed time (ميقات) with his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic—men whose faith and sincerity were unquestionable. Yet, his choice fell upon hypocrites. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَاخْتارَ مُوسى قَوْمَهُ سَبْعِينَ رَجُلاً لِمِيقاتِنا ** (And Moses chose seventy men from his people for Our tryst) up to His saying: لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ (we will not believe you until we see Allah manifestly. Thereupon the thunderbolt seized you) due to their wrongdoing. When we found that the choice of one whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had chosen for prophethood fell upon the most corrupt (الأفسد) instead of the most righteous (الأصلح), while he thought he was the most righteous instead of the most corrupt, we knew that choosing is not permissible except by the One Who knows what the breasts conceal, what the consciences harbor, and to Whom the secrets are directed. And that there is no weight (خطر) to the choice of the Muhajirun and the Ansar, after the choice of the Prophets fell upon those who possessed corruption, even though they intended righteousness."

I say: These narratives indicate the first interpretation of the Ayah.

As for the second interpretation, it is indicated by what was narrated in Misbah al-Shari'ah from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a discourse of His, where He said: "And you know that the forelocks (نواصي) of creation are in His Hand, so they do not have a breath or a moment except by His power and His will, and they are incapable of bringing about the least thing in His dominion except by His permission and His desire." The Almighty Allah said: وَرَبُّكَ يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ وَيَخْتارُ (Your Lord creates whatever He wishes and chooses) [the rest of] the Ayah.

(69)** وَرَبُّكَ يَعْلَمُ ما تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ وَما يُعْلِنُونَ ** (69) But your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they disclose.

Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] what they resolved upon regarding the choice (الاختيار).

I say: And according to the first interpretation, it is permissible that the meaning is: وَرَبُّكَ ** is He Who يَعْلَمُ ما ** the breasts conceal and what the consciences hide, exclusively, without others. Thus, it is for Him to choose for prophethood (النبوّة), Imamate (الإمامة), and other things besides them, not for them. And perhaps this meaning was alluded to at the end of the Hadith al-Ikmal (the Narration of Perfection) by His saying: We know that the choice (الاختيار) is not permissible to be made except by the One Who knows what the breasts hide, what the consciences conceal, and to Whom the secrets turn.

(٧٠) وَهُوَ اللهُ ** (70) And He is Allah, the One deserving of worship (العبادة). لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ** there is no god except Him. No one deserves it except Him. لَهُ الْحَمْدُ فِي الْأُولى وَالْآخِرَةِ ** To Him belongs all praise in the former and the latter [lives]. because He is the Master (المولى) of all blessings (النّعم), both immediate and deferred. The believers praise Him in the Hereafter just as they praised Him in the world, by their saying: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا الْحَزَنَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي صَدَقَنا وَعْدَهُ ** (All praise belongs to Allah who has removed grief from us. All praise belongs to Allah who has made good His promise to us.) rejoicing in His favor and delighting in praising Him. وَلَهُ الْحُكْمُ ** and to Him belongs the judgment, the decisive decree (القضاء النّافذ) concerning everything. وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ ** and to Him you shall be brought back. through the Resurrection (بالنّشور).

(٧١) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ جَعَلَ اللهُ عَلَيْكُمُ اللَّيْلَ سَرْمَداً إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ مَنْ إِلهٌ غَيْرُ اللهِ يَأْتِيكُمْ بِضِياءٍ أَفَلا تَسْمَعُونَ ** (71) Say, 'Tell me, if Allah were to make the night perpetual for you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than Allah would bring you light? Will you not listen?' (meaning) a listening of reflection (تدبّر) and insight (استبصار). (٧٢) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ جَعَلَ اللهُ عَلَيْكُمُ النَّهارَ سَرْمَداً إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ مَنْ إِلهٌ غَيْرُ اللهِ يَأْتِيكُمْ بِلَيْلٍ تَسْكُنُونَ فِيهِ ** (72) Say, 'Tell me, if Allah were to make the day perpetual for you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than Allah would bring you night in which you may rest? (meaning) a rest from the hardships of work (الأشغال) أَفَلا تُبْصِرُونَ ** Will you not perceive?' And perhaps He did not describe the light (الضّياء) with what corresponds to it (i.e., rest/tranquility) because light (الضّوء) is a blessing in itself, intended for its own sake, and the night is not like that. And because the benefits of light (الضّوء) are greater than what corresponds to it (i.e., the night). For this reason, He coupled with it أَفَلا تَسْمَعُونَ ** (Will you not listen?) and with the night أَفَلا تُبْصِرُونَ ** (Will you not perceive?), because the mind's benefit from hearing (السّمع) is greater than its benefit from sight (البصر).

(٧٣) ** وَمِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ جَعَلَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ لِتَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ ** (73) And it is out of His mercy that He made for you the night and the day, that you may rest in it (The resting is) in the night, وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ في النَّهارِ (and that you may seek His bounty in the day) by means of various kinds of earnings (المكاسب), وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (and that you may be grateful). And so that you may recognize the favor of Allah in that, and thus thank Him for it.

(٧٤)** وَيَوْمَ يُنادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكائِيَ الَّذِينَ كُنْتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ ** (74) And the day He calls out to them, and says, 'Where are My partners whom you used to claim?'

(This is) a reproach after a reproach, to indicate that nothing draws the wrath of Allah more than associating partners (الاشراك) with Him. And because the first (reproach) was for establishing the corruption of their view, while the second is for clarifying that it was not based on any proof (برهان).

(٧٥) وَنَزَعْنا And We brought forth (وأخرجنا) مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيداً who bears witness against them concerning what they were upon. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: [It means] from every group (فرقة) of this community (الأمة) their Imam. Then We said to the communities: هاتُوا بُرْهانَكُمْ concerning the validity of what you held as religion. فَعَلِمُوا at that time (حينئذ) أَنَّ الْحَقَّ لِلَّهِ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ And it vanished from them, the vanishing of something lost (غيبة الضّايع), ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ of falsehood.

** وَنَزَعْنا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيداً فَقُلْنا هاتُوا بُرْهانَكُمْ فَعَلِمُوا أَنَّ الْحَقَّ لِلَّهِ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** (75) And We shall draw a witness from every nation, and We shall say, 'Produce your evidence.' Then they will know that the truth belongs to Allah, and what they used to fabricate will forsake them.

(٧٦) إِنَّ قارُونَ كانَ مِنْ قَوْمِ مُوسى ** (76) Qarun was one of the people of Moses It was said that he was his cousin, the son of Yishar ibn Qahath ibn Lawi, and he was among those who believed in him (Moses). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was his maternal cousin (ابن خالته), and there is no contradiction between the two reports. فَبَغى عَلَيْهِمْ (and he acted tyrannically towards them), meaning he sought superiority over them and became arrogant (فتكبّر). وَآتَيْناهُ مِنَ الْكُنُوزِ (and We gave him such treasures), meaning from the stored wealth (الأموال المدّخرة), ما إِنَّ مَفاتِحَهُ (that its keys)—the keys to his chests (مفاتيح صنادقه), which is the plural of miftah (مفتح) with a kasrah— لَتَنُوأُ بِالْعُصْبَةِ ** would weigh down a band due to the heaviness of the large group (الجماعة الكثيرة) أُولِي الْقُوَّةِ (of strong men). In al-Qummi: al-'Usbah (العصبيّة) is between ten and nineteen men. He said: The band (العصبة) of strong men (أولوا القوّة) used to carry the keys to his treasuries. إِذْ قالَ لَهُ قَوْمُهُ لا تَفْرَحْ ** (when his people said to him, 'Do not exult), meaning: do not be boastful (لا تبطر). إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْفَرِحِينَ ** (Indeed Allah does not like the exultant ones), meaning those who exult in the adornments (زخارف) of the world. In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them both): Allah Almighty revealed to Moses (peace be upon him): "Do not exult in the abundance of wealth, and do not abandon My remembrance (ذكري) in any state, for the abundance of My remembrance makes sins forgotten (تنسي الذّنوب), and abandoning My remembrance hardens the hearts (يقسي القلوب)." And in al-Tawhid, narrated from him (peace be upon him): If the punishment (العقوبة) from Allah Almighty is true, then why the exultation (فالفرح لما ذا)?

(٧٧) وَابْتَغِ فِيما آتاكَ اللهُ ** من الغنى الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ ** بصرفه فيما يوجبها لك وَلا تَنْسَ ** ولا تَتْرك نَصِيبَكَ مِنَ الدُّنْيا (77) And seek, in what Allah has given you of wealth, the abode of the Hereafter by spending it in what necessitates it for you, and do not forget (or neglect) your share of the world.**

في المعاني عنه عن أبيه عن جدّه عن أمير المؤمنين عليهم السلام قال : لا تَنْسَ ** صحّتك وقوّتك وفراغك وشبابك ونشاطك ان تطلب بها الآخرة وَأَحْسِنْ الى عباد الله كَما أَحْسَنَ اللهُ إِلَيْكَ ** فيما أنعم عليك او أَحْسِنْ الشكر والطاعة كَما أَحْسَنَ اللهُ إِلَيْكَ ** بالانعام وَلا تَبْغِ الْفَسادَ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُفْسِدِينَ. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from him, from his father, from his grandfather, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them) who said: "Do not forget" your health, your strength, your free time, your youth, and your vigor, that you may seek the Hereafter through them. And be good to the servants of Allah "just as Allah has been good to you" in what He has bestowed upon you. Or, be good in gratitude (الشكر) and obedience (الطاعة) "just as Allah has been good to you" through the bestowal of favors (بالانعام). "and do not seek corruption in the earth. Indeed, Allah does not like the corruptors."**

في مصباح الشريعة قال الصادق عليه السلام : فساد الظاهر من فساد الباطن ومن أصلح سريرته أصلح الله علانيته ومن خان الله في السّر هتك الله سرّه في العلانية وأعظم الفساد ان يرضى العبد بالغفلة عن الله تعالى إذ هذا الفساد يتولّد من طول الأمد والحرص والكبر كما اخبر الله تعالى في قصّة قارون في قوله وَلا تَبْغِ الْفَسادَ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** وكانت هذه الخصال من صنع قارون واعتقاده وأصلها من حبّ الدنيا وجمعها ومتابعة النفس وهواها وإقامة شهواتها وحبّ المحمدة وموافقة الشيطان واتّباع خطراته وكلّ ذلك مجتمع تحت الغفلة عن الله ونسيان منّته. In Misbah al-Shari'ah, Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: The corruption of the outward (الظاهر) stems from the corruption of the inward (الباطن). And whoever rectifies his inner self (سريرته), Allah rectifies his outward self (علانيته). And whoever betrays Allah in secret, Allah exposes his secret in public. And the greatest corruption is for the servant to be content with heedlessness (الغفلة) toward Allah, the Almighty, since this corruption is generated by prolonged hope (طول الأمد), greed (الحرص), and arrogance (الكبر), as Allah, the Almighty, informed in the story of Qarun in His saying: وَلا تَبْغِ الْفَسادَ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُفْسِدِينَ ** (and do not seek corruption in the earth. Indeed, Allah does not like the corruptors.) And these characteristics were part of Qarun's actions and belief, and their root is the love of the world (الدنيا) and gathering it, and following the soul (النفس) and its caprice (هواها), and establishing its desires (شهواتها), and the love of praise (المحمدة), and agreeing with Satan (الشيطان) and following his suggestions (خطراته). And all of that is gathered under heedlessness (الغفلة) toward Allah and forgetting His favor (منّته).

(٧٨) قالَ إِنَّما أُوتِيتُهُ عَلى عِلْمٍ عندي. (78) He said, 'I have been given it only because of a knowledge that I have.' Al-Qummi said: Meaning, his wealth, and he used to practice alchemy (الكيمياء). أَوَلَمْ يَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللهَ قَدْ أَهْلَكَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ مِنَ الْقُرُونِ مَنْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُ قُوَّةً وَأَكْثَرُ جَمْعاً وَلا يُسْئَلُ عَنْ ذُنُوبِهِمُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ ** Did he not know that Allah had destroyed before him generations who were mightier than him in power and greater in accumulation? The guilty will not be questioned about their sins. القمّيّ لا يسئل من كان قبلهم عن ذنوب هؤلاء. Al-Qummi said: Those who were before them will not be questioned about the sins of these people.

(٧٩) فَخَرَجَ عَلى قَوْمِهِ فِي زِينَتِهِ ** (79) (So he went out before his people in his finery.) Al-Qummi (narrated) that (he went out) in dyed garments, dragging them along the ground. And it was said that he went out upon a grayish-white mule, wearing purple (الأرجوان), and upon (the mule) was a saddle of gold, and with him were four thousand (men) in his attire (زية). قالَ الَّذِينَ يُرِيدُونَ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا ** (Said those who desired the life of the world,) according to the custom (عادة) of people in their desire for it. يا لَيْتَ لَنا مِثْلَ ما أُوتِيَ قارُونُ ** (‘O, would that we had the like of what Qārūn has been given!) They wished for the like of it, not the exact thing itself, out of caution against envy (الحسد). إِنَّهُ لَذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ (Indeed, he is a man of great fortune) in the world.

(٨٠) ** وَقالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ ** (80) And those who were given knowledge said (They said this) regarding the states of the Hereafter, to those who were wishing (for Qarun's wealth). Al-Qummi said: The sincere ones (الخالص) among the companions of Musa said to them: وَيْلَكُمْ ثَوابُ اللهِ خَيْرٌ لِمَنْ آمَنَ Woe to you! Allah’s reward is better for someone who has faith وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً ** and acts righteously than what Qarun was given, rather, (it is better) than the world and what is in it. وَلا يُلَقَّاها ** meaning, this word (الكلمة) which the scholars spoke **إِلَّا الصَّابِرُونَ ** except the patient. (The patient ones are those who are patient) in performing acts of obedience and in refraining from acts of disobedience.

(٨١) ** فَخَسَفْنا بِهِ وَبِدارِهِ الْأَرْضَ** (81) So We caused the earth to swallow him and his house.

In Manahi al-Faqih (مناهي الفقيه): And He forbade a man from being arrogant (يَخْتَال) in his walk, and He said: "Whoever wears a garment and is arrogant (اخْتَالَ) in it, Allah will cause the earth to swallow him from the edge (شَفِير) of Hell, and he will be the companion (قَرِين) of Qarun, because he was the first to be arrogant, so Allah caused the earth to swallow him and his house."

فَما كانَ لَهُ مِنْ فِئَةٍ (81) Then he had no host (helpers)

**يَنْصُرُونَهُ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (81) to help him against Allah, who could aid him and thus repel His punishment from him.

**وَما كانَ مِنَ المُنْتَصِرِينَ ** (81) nor was he one of those who could defend themselves. (meaning) those who could prevent it (the punishment). It is narrated: That Moses (peace be upon him) passed by him with his family, his brother Harun, and his sons, and the earth swallowed him, his family, his wealth, and those of his people who supported him.

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ): The reason for Qarun's destruction was that when Moses (peace be upon him) brought the Children of Israel out of Egypt and settled them in the desert (الْبَادِيَة), Allah sent down al-Mann and al-Salwa (المنّ والسلوى) upon them. Until he said: Then Allah obligated them to enter Egypt and forbade it to them for forty years. They used to rise at the beginning of the night and engage in reading the Torah (التوراة), supplication (الدعاء), and weeping. Qarun was among them, and he used to read the Torah, and there was no one among them with a better voice than him. He was called al-Manun (المنون) due to the beauty of his recitation. He also practiced alchemy (الكيمياء). When the matter became prolonged for the Children of Israel in the wandering (التيه) and repentance (التوبة), Qarun refused to join them in repentance. Moses (peace be upon him) loved him, so Moses entered upon him and said to him: "O Qarun, your people are in repentance, and you are sitting here. Join them, or the punishment will descend upon you." But he belittled him and mocked his saying. So Moses departed from him grieved. He sat in the courtyard (فِنَاء) of Qarun's palace, wearing a woolen cloak (جُبَّة شَعْر) and on his feet were two sandals made of donkey hide, their straps (شِرَاكَهُمَا) made of threads of hair, and in his hand was the staff (الْعَصَا). Qarun ordered that ashes mixed with water be poured upon him, so it was poured upon him. Moses (peace be upon him) became intensely angry. He had hairs on His shoulder that, when He became angry, would emerge from His clothes, and blood would drip from them. Moses (peace be upon him) said: "O Lord, if You do not become angry for my sake, then I am not a prophet for You." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to Him: "I have commanded the earth to obey you, so command it whatever you wish." Qarun had previously ordered the palace gate to be locked. Moses approached and gestured toward the doors, and they opened, and He entered upon him. When Qarun looked at Him, he knew that the punishment had come. He said: "O Moses, I ask you by the kinship (الرَّحِم) that is between me and you." Moses said to him: "O son of Lawi (لاوى), do not increase your words to me! O Earth, take him!" So the palace, with everything in it, sank into the earth. Qarun sank into the earth up to his knees. He wept and swore by the kinship (الرَّحِم). Moses said to him: "O son of Lawi, do not increase your words to me! O Earth, take him!" So it swallowed him with his palace and his treasures. This is what Moses said to Qarun the day Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, destroyed him. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, reproached Him for what He had said to Qarun. Moses (peace be upon him) knew that Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, had reproached Him for that, so He said: "O Lord, Qarun called upon me by other than You, and had he called upon You, I would have answered him." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "O son of Lawi, do not increase your words to me!" Moses said: "O Lord, had I known that this was pleasing to You, I would have answered him." Allah said: "O Moses, by My Might (عِزَّتِي), My Majesty (جَلَالِي), My Generosity (جُودِي), My Glory (مَجْدِي), and the loftiness of My station (عُلُوّ مَكَانِي), had Qarun called upon Me as he called upon you, I would have answered Him. But when he called upon you, I entrusted him to you. O son of Imran, do not despair of death, for I have decreed death upon every soul, and I have prepared a resting place (مِهَادًا) for you that, were you to arrive at it, your eyes would be cooled." So Moses (peace be upon him) went out to Mount Tur Sinai (طور سيناء) with His successor (وَصِيّه). Moses (peace be upon him) ascended the mountain and looked at a man who approached, carrying a basket (مِكْتَل) and a spade (مِسْحَاة). Moses (peace be upon him) said to him: "What do you want?" He said: "A man from the friends of Allah (أَوْلِيَاء الله) has died, and I am digging a grave for him." Moses (peace be upon him) said to him: "Shall I not help you with it?" He said: "Yes." So He dug the grave. When they finished, the man intended to descend into the grave. Moses said to him: "What do you want?" He said: "I will enter the grave and see how his resting place is." Moses said to him: "I will suffice you." So Moses entered and lay down in it. Then the Angel of Death (مَلَك الْمَوْت) seized His soul, and the mountain closed upon Him.

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) in Surat Yunus: Some Jews asked the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) about a prison that traveled the corners (أَقْطَار) of the earth with its occupant. He said: "O Jew, as for the prison that traveled the corners of the earth with its occupant, it is the whale (الْحُوت) that imprisoned Yunus in its belly. It entered the Sea of Qulzum (بَحْر الْقُلْزُم), then exited to the Sea of Egypt (بَحْر مِصْر), then entered the Sea of Tabaristan (بَحْر طَبَرِسْتَان), then exited into the Tigris of al-Ghawr (دِجْلَة الْغَوْر)." He said: "Then it passed beneath the earth until it reached Qarun. Qarun had perished in the days of Moses, and Allah had appointed an angel over him who would sink him into the earth the height of a man every day. Yunus was in the belly of the whale glorifying Allah (يُسَبِّحُ الله) and seeking His forgiveness (يَسْتَغْفِرُهُ). Qarun heard his voice and said to the appointed angel: 'Grant me respite (انْظُرْنِي), for I hear the speech of a human being (آدَمِيّ).' So Allah revealed to the appointed angel: 'Grant him respite.' So he granted him respite. Then Qarun said: 'Who are you?' He said: 'I am Yunus, the sinful, erring one, Yunus son of Matta.' He said: 'What happened to Moses son of Imran, the one intensely angry for the sake of Allah?' He said: 'Alas, he perished.' He said: 'What happened to Harun son of Imran, the compassionate and merciful toward his people?' He said: 'He perished.' He said: 'What happened to Kulthum bint Imran, who was named for me?' He said: 'Alas, no one remains from the family of Imran.' So Qarun said: 'Woe (أَسَفًا) upon the family of Imran!' Allah, the Exalted, thanked him for that, so He commanded the appointed angel to lift the punishment from him for the remaining days of the world. So the punishment was lifted from him." The narrative, and its completion, will come in Surat al-Saffat.

And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي) narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: When Yunus (peace be upon him) was harmed by his people—and He continued the narrative until He said—so he cast himself (into the sea)

**فَالْتَقَمَهُ الْحُوتُ ** (42) and the fish swallowed him.

So it traveled with him through the seven seas until it reached al-Bahr al-Masjur (الْبَحْر الْمَسْجُور), and in it Qarun was being punished. Qarun heard a rumbling sound (دَوِيًّا) and asked the angel about it. He informed him that it was Yunus and that Allah had imprisoned him in the belly of the whale. Qarun said to him: "Will you permit me to speak to him?" So he permitted him. He asked him about Moses, and he informed him that He had died, so he wept. Then he asked him about Harun (peace be upon him), and he informed him that He had died, so he wept and grieved intensely (جَزَعًا شَدِيدًا). He asked him about his sister Kulthum, who had been named for him, and he informed him that she had died, so he wept and grieved intensely. He (Imam al-Baqir) said: Then Allah revealed to the appointed angel: "Lift the punishment from him for the remainder of the days of the world, due to his tenderness (رِقَّتِهِ) toward his kinship."

(٨٢) وَأَصْبَحَ الَّذِينَ تَمَنَّوْا مَكانَهُ (his status) بِالْأَمْسِ ** (since a short time ago) يَقُولُونَ وَيْكَأَنَّ اللهَ (Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said it is a Syriac language) يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ (according to the requirement of His Will, not due to an honor that necessitates expansion, nor due to a disgrace that necessitates restriction) (82) And those who wished for his place yesterday began to say, 'Woe! As if Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants, and restricts it. لَوْ لا أَنْ مَنَّ اللهُ عَلَيْنا ** (and thus did not give us what we wished for) لَخَسَفَ بِنا ** (because of the generation within us of what He generated within him, for which reason He made the earth swallow him). And it was read with fathah on the kha and the sin. He would have made the earth swallow us. وَيْكَأَنَّهُ لا يُفْلِحُ الْكافِرُونَ ** (regarding the grace of Allah). Woe! As if the faithless do not prosper.'

٨٣ ** تِلْكَ الدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ ** (That is the abode of the Hereafter) which you have heard news of and whose description has reached you. نَجْعَلُها لِلَّذِينَ لا يُرِيدُونَ عُلُوًّا فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (which We shall assign to those who do not seek to dominate in the land) meaning, dominance (غلبة) and subjugation (قهراً). وَلا فَساداً ** (nor to cause corruption.) meaning, injustice (ظلماً) against the people.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He used to walk in the markets while He was the governor (وال), guiding the lost and helping the weak. He would pass by the seller (البيّاع) and the grocer (البقّال), open the Quran to them, and recite this Ayah, saying: "It was revealed concerning the people of justice (العدل) and humility (التواضع) among the rulers (الولاة) and the people of power (القدرة) among the rest of mankind." And narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "A man may be pleased with the strap (شراك) of his sandal (نعله), and thus he enters into [the scope of] this Ayah." And in another narration: "A man may be pleased that the strap of his sandal is better than the strap of his companion's sandal, and thus he falls under [the scope of] it." And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): al-'Uluww (العلوّ) is honor (الشّرف), and al-Fasad (الفساد) is building (النّباء).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He said to Hafs ibn Ghiyath (حفص بن غياث): "O Hafs, the status of the world to my soul is only like the status of a dead carcass (الميتة); if I am compelled [by necessity] toward it, I eat from it. O Hafs, verily Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, knew what the servants would do (عاملون) and to what they would return (صايرون). So, He was forbearing toward them concerning their evil deeds due to His prior knowledge concerning them. Therefore, do not be deceived by the good seeking (حسن الطلب) of one who does not fear missing [the opportunity]." Then He recited His saying: تِلْكَ الدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ ** [the rest of] the Ayah, and He began to weep, saying: "By Allah, hopes (الاماني) vanished upon [hearing] this Ayah! By Allah, the righteous (الأبرار) have triumphed! Do you know who they are? They are those who do not harm even an ant (الذرّ). Knowledge suffices in the fear of Allah (خشية الله), and ignorance suffices in being deceived by Allah (الاغترار بالله)." [End of] the narration (الحديث).

وَالْعاقِبَةُ ** (and the outcome) the praiseworthy (المحمودة) outcome. لِلْمُتَّقِينَ ** (is for the Godwary.) those who guard against what Allah does not approve of.

(84) ** مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ خَيْرٌ مِنْها ** (84) Whoever brings virtue shall receive better than it. in essence (ذاتاً), in measure (وقدراً), and in description (ووصفاً). And a narration (حديث) concerning this verse has already passed at the end of Surah al-An'am and concerning its parallel (نظيرها) at the end of Surah an-Naml. وَمَنْ جاءَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ فَلا يُجْزَى الَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا السَّيِّئاتِ ** But whoever brings vice—those who commit vices shall not be requited In this [phrase], the explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (الضمير) in order to condemn (تهجينا) their state by repeatedly attributing the vice (السيّئة) to them. إِلَّا ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** except for what they used to do. [It means] like ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (what they used to do). The word 'like' (المثل) was omitted (حذف) for emphasis (مبالغة) in the similarity (المماثلة).

٨٥ ** إِنَّ الَّذِي فَرَضَ عَلَيْكَ الْقُرْآنَ لَرادُّكَ إِلى مَعادٍ ** (Indeed He who has obligated the Quran upon you will surely bring you back to a destination.) What destination (أيّ معاد).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him) who said: "Your Prophet, the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and the Imams (peace be upon them) will return to you."

Narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Jabir was mentioned in His presence, and He said: "May Allah have mercy upon Jabir. His knowledge had reached the extent that he knew the interpretation (تأويل) of this verse," meaning the Return (الرّجعة).

قُلْ رَبِّي أَعْلَمُ مَنْ جاءَ بِالْهُدى وَمَنْ هُوَ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ ** (Say, 'My Lord knows best him who brings guidance and him who is in manifest error.') He means by it Himself and the polytheists (المشركين).

(٨٦) وَما كُنْتَ تَرْجُوا أَنْ يُلْقى إِلَيْكَ الْكِتابُ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (86) You did not expect the Book to be cast upon you, but it is a mercy from your Lord. ولكن ألقاه رحمة منه But He cast it down as a mercy from Him. فَلا تَكُونَنَّ ظَهِيراً لِلْكافِرِينَ ** So do not be a backer of the faithless. It was said by showing courtesy (بمداراتهم), bearing their burdens (والتحمّل عنهم), and responding to their requests (والإجابة الى طلبتهم). And al-Qummi said that the address is directed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the meaning is for the people (للنّاس).

(٨٧) وَلا يَصُدُّنَّكَ عَنْ آياتِ اللهِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أُنْزِلَتْ إِلَيْكَ وَادْعُ إِلى رَبِّكَ to His worship and His Oneness (توحيده) وَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ. (87) And do not let them bar you from the signs of Allah after they have been sent down to you, and invite [people] to your Lord, and do not be one of the polytheists. (٨٨) وَلا تَدْعُ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ. (88) And do not invoke another god besides Allah. Al-Qummi (said): The address is to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, but the meaning is for the people. This is the saying of Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Indeed, Allah sent His Prophet with [the principle]: 'I mean you, so listen, O neighbor (إِيَّاكَ أَعْنِي وَاسْمَعِي يَا جَارَة).'" لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ. There is no god except Him. Everything is perishable except His Face. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "What is meant by that is the Face (وجه) of Allah through Whom He is approached (يؤتى منه)." And in al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is greater than to be described by the Face (الوجه). Rather, its meaning is: كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هالِكٌ إِلَّا ** His religion (دينه) and the Face (الوجه) through Whom He is approached (يؤتى منه)." I say: What is meant by the Face (الوجه) through Whom He is approached (يؤتى منه) is that which guides the servants to Allah the Almighty and to His knowledge, whether it be a Prophet (نبيّ), or a Successor (وصيّ), or a perfect intellect (عقل كامل) that is faithful (وفيّ) in that regard. For that is the Face (وجه) of Allah through Whom Allah is approached (يؤتى الله منه). This is because the face (الوجه) is that by which one confronts (يواجه), and Allah, the Glorified, only confronts His servants and addresses them through the medium of a Prophet (نبيّ), or a Successor (وصيّ), or a perfect intellect (عقل كامل). And in al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, He said: كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هالِكٌ إِلَّا ** "He who takes the path of truth." And narrated from Him عليه السلام: "He who approaches Allah with what He commanded, namely the obedience of Muhammad and the Imams (may the prayers of Allah be upon them) after him, he is the Face (الوجه) that does not perish." Then He recited: مَنْ يُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطاعَ اللهَ (Whoever obeys the Apostle has certainly obeyed Allah). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him عليه السلام, is that which is similar in meaning: "The intent is that every person obedient to Allah and His Messenger, who is turned toward Allah, will remain in the Gardens (الجنان) forever and ever. And he is the Face (وجه) of Allah among His creation, through whom Allah the Almighty confronts His servants. And whoever is contrary to this will be in the Fires (النيران) with the perished ones (الهالكين). And the recitation of the Ayah is an indication that his obedience to the Messenger is a turning (توجّه) from him toward Allah and toward His Face (وجهه), and a turning (توجّه) from Allah the Almighty toward His creation, and this is the reason for naming him the Face (وجه) of Allah and attributing it to Him." And in al-Tawhid, narrated from Him عليه السلام: "We are the Face (وجه) of Allah that does not perish." And narrated from Him عليه السلام regarding إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ : He said: "His religion (دينه). And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام were the religion (دين) of Allah, His Face (وجهه), and His Eye (عينه) among His servants, and His Tongue (لسانه) with which He speaks, and His Hand (يده) over His creation. And we are the Face (وجه) of Allah through Whom He is approached (يؤتى منه). We shall not cease to be among His servants as long as Allah has a 'vision' (رؤية) concerning them." It was asked: "And what is the 'vision' (الرّؤية)?" He replied: "The need (الحاجة). So, if Allah has no need (حاجة) concerning them, He will raise us to Him and do with us what He loves." And Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding this Ayah: He said: "Everything will perish, and the Face (الوجه) will remain. Allah is greater than to be described, but its meaning is: كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هالِكٌ إِلَّا ** His religion (دينه). And we are the Face (الوجه) through Whom He is approached (يؤتى منه). We shall not cease to be among His servants." [Allah is] more glorious (اجلّ) and greater (أعظم) than that. And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: "The intent is كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هالِكٌ إِلَّا دينه (Everything is perishable except His religion), because it is impossible (المحال) that everything perishes from Him and the Face (الوجه) remains as He is." And He mentioned something similar to what is in al-Tawhid: "He who is not from Him perishes. Do you not see that He said: كُلُّ مَنْ عَلَيْها فانٍ وَيَبْقى وَجْهُ رَبِّكَ ** (Everyone on it is bound to perish, (26) but the Face of your Lord will remain)?" Thus, He distinguished between His creation and His Face (وجهه). I say: And it was narrated in another tradition from them عليهم السلام that the pronoun in وَجْهَهُ ** refers back to the 'thing' (الشيء). According to this, its meaning is that the face (وجه) of the thing does not perish, and that is what turns from it toward Allah, which is its spirit (روحه), its reality (حقيقته), its dominion (ملكوته), and the locus of the knowledge of Allah within it, which remains after the perishing of its body (جسمه) and its person (شخصه). And the two meanings are close. And sometimes the Face (الوجه) is interpreted as the Essence (الذّات), and this is not far-fetched. لَهُ الْحُكْمُ ** (To Him belongs the judgment) — the decisive decree (القضاء النّافذ) concerning creation — وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ ** (and to Him you shall be brought back) — for the just recompense (للجزاء بالحقّ). The reward for reciting this Surah was previously mentioned at the end of Surah al-Shu'ara.
